top_profane.text: Complete trash. The screw holes aren’t milled well at all which mean partially driven screws. That wouldn’t be a complete deal breaker except the screws are so soft that they immediately strip out. Was unable to get the case screwed together, but now am unable to get one of the screws out because it stripped so easily. Now instead of a crappy screen I have two unplayable scrap heaps. Well worth $80 bucks. Oh and BTW a BUNCH of the other kits come with electronics screw drivers. Not this one. So you will spend an afternoon getting the right tools, and  Only then do you get the blessed opportunity to create scrap heaps. I am unable to fully convey how pissed off I am. You want 80 bucks, seller? Then put a penny or two into finishing your milling properly and providing screws that aren’t made of wax. (But really, I shouldn’t have to go make the holes bigger to get the screws in. Screw you guys!) top_profane.text:
Operation Flashpoint 2 Review
Overview
I am going to be really frank on my opinion of this game, it is complete and utter garbage. Now I tried giving the game a second chance, I did, and at first I was like, hmm well maybe this isn't so bad and then boom, this is garbage.
Now it wasn't the realism that made me not like the game, in fact that part I loved. It was the unrealism that killed this one and made it utter crap. You play as ether 2nd lieutenant Hidaigo (IE the good missions) and Sergeant Hunter (IE the utter garbage missions). The Hidaigo is more spec ops missions and Hunter is more front line missions. The difference is that with Hidaigo you can get some enjoyment out of the game and the later missions for Hunter all of the games issues compound to make a literally unenjoyable experience. Now I love video games so I get some enjoyment from this just because it is a video game but if you are not like me then I doubt you will find any enjoyment from this.
Some of the issues that this game has in the unrealistic department are, to begin with the fact that you can't shoot for crap and you're a marine. Now I don't know the exact details on how good they can shoot but I am pretty sure that they put bullets on tangos (enemies) like flies on stank. Instead you get to play as a guy that can't shoot for crap and you would swear just got out of boot camp. In the attempt to make it look realistic the developers created a nightmare with this where you could aim steadily, empty your clip, and still not have killed the guy let alone hit him (I in fact did have this happen to me at a distance which I should have been able to kill him instantly.
Another issue are the enemies health, which unlike the way you have it where one bullet equals potential death, they can take round after round in typical video game health. Issue with this it isn't realistic. I counted how many rounds it took to take a guy down, nine, nine rounds single fire to kill a guy. Now I am not a soldier so I can only assume here, but I am pretty sure after the 5 bullet your arse would be on the ground but not that guy.
A newer and later issue I found in the game is random explosions. Gone is the need for a rockets smoke trail and now replaced by unrealistic death. As my brother watched a rocket went right pass my humvee a complete miss by a foot and then the humvee exploded. I am not kidding on that ether, and worse your rockets don't hit crap for crap. They will veer off and smack the ground instead of their intended targets. Yours are also slow as all crap as revealed to me when I was trying to take out an attack helicopter in one mission which I didn't succeed at as my rockets were to slow and the helicopter could just move out of the way like the rockets were nothing. You can't kill the helicopter pilot with gun fire ether (something that would be realistic) by shooting into the cockpit nor can you shoot the helicopter down with bullets ether (damaging parts not just bang, bang, boom).
Worst of all is the AI which is only slightly above Halo AI (for those that don't know the marines in halo are butt freaking stupid). They can't do a freaking thing right, which kills your ability to effectively flank or do anything worthwhile. They don't duck when they need to, they don't follow you over a simple barricade (specifically happened), and they will stand occasionally when you are downed right in front of you and not heal you after you have told them to do so. Basically they are about useless except for the fact they can shoot enemies.
Now the story while interesting will not have you wanting to fight. It is about oil, which is made abundantly clear. Sure you are also attempting to prevent a major war between Russia and China but when you think about it, your actions are only going to make the Chinese hate the US and just worsen matters.

Like: Some of the realistic aspects, early missions are enjoyable and fun
Dislike: Dumb AI, Unrealistic aspects of the game, Plot isn't making me actually want to fight, Enemy rockets, the forceful rush of some missions.

Gameplay
Okay for game play think Ghost Recon on Hard and then crap it up. You go about completing objectives from any of the 11 missions as you unlock them, the latter of which are completely awful. You do this as if you are Ghosts (from ghost recon) and in a poor attempt at making you feel like part of a bigger movement often times you will fight with others who if are killed (which happens easily) you loose. These others often times will not wait to advance until after you clear the anti tank units, but instead rush right ahead and get themselves killed and your mission is scrapped and you have to do the whole thing over again or from a check point.
Now when you are fighting you have a nice 25 kilometer island to explo... no wait scratch that. You have a long arse way to walk during missions which only take place is small parts of the island and often time will just be rushed into failure. Open worldness might have saved this game, as it would have added fun. Avoid that ambush, stalk around a road block and hit it from the rear after careful planning and great execution, the possibilities would be endless and would have thrown it much fun.
You command a 3 man team, sort of. The AI is so bad that it doesn't really work for crap. While you can use the commands to some extent, the AI of your men completely hamper any attempt at a strategy.
If you get injured you will start to bleed out or that is what it is supposed to be, instead you have a little red circle begin depleting at a decent rate until you are dead. You use field dressing to stop this bleed out and do the same for you men. This consists of holding a button and waiting for a circle to fill. What would have been nice to see would have been something like the health system from MGS3 (Metal Gear Solid 3). That would have added some dimension to the game.

Graphics
Graphics are on a decent level. The island does actually look pretty good. Not up close where the graphics fail miserably as they are your usual cookie cutter trees, and grass and bushes, but as a whole they look pretty good.
Vehicles look good, and people do as well. Not much really to say in for graphics in this game, it doesn't do anything that will wow you but at the same time it doesn't do much that is bad ether. Average is the word to use for the games graphics.

Presentation
Well the story will not inspire you to fight, the manual is not half bad but I have seen better, and the game itself only meets half way. The menus are pretty good, you can view your equipment (not change it), you can get a full briefing on why your are about to go and do whatever the mission requires and view the map of the area in which you will be carrying out the operation. All in all, not bad but not a highlight ether.

Value
If you want to play this game I would go used or discounted. It is not worth 60 dollars and will bring much frustration. Instead pick it up at around 30 dollars or Rent it.

Fun Level: 2/5

Closing Statement
The game didn't disappoint me because I didn't have an opinion on how good or bad it was going to be but in the end the game proved that it was more bad than good. Maybe a few patches down the line this game can redeem itself but as for now this game is garbage. The frustration level from just crap in the game is too high for the enemies don't have to follow the restrictions you do. They can take more bullets, they can aim better, and their rockets magically kill you. On top of that in an attempt to increase tension they try rushing the player with an urgency by making convoys move up, helicopter arriving in a few minutes, ext. Yet in the end this does not improve game play and ultimately takes away from the enjoyment factor they player is seeking and when you have so far to walk, to throw in time restrictions is a bad idea.
Final Verdict -1/-5 (1 to 5 is a rating of how good the game is, -1 through -5 is a rating of how bad)
I can't in all honesty recommend this game. If you must play it, rent it.

Parental Warning
The game contains blood, violence, weapons, and scenes of war. Parental discretion is advised for this title.

Check out this review and more at [...]
top_profane.title: Operation owe so close!

top_profane.text:
Read my updates below.
Unless you like playing single games do not get this crap. If you are a longtime Franchise mode player do not get this....there is NO Franchise mode and what we have in it's place...Crappy Careers...is not Franchise mode and it's crap. The new game engine is a nice idea but it's far from ready and therefore it's crap. Maybe if EA had concentrated on the game play engine and left the rest of what they had in M12 alone they would have had a game but what they gave us is crap and I refuse to call it a game because it's just crap. I hope I'm getting my point across with my continuous use of the word crap...M13 truly is a POS.

09/01/12: Now that I have time to play more I just want to add this to my previous comments. At first glance I thought that the new onfield game play was better but after playing 2 seasons worth of games I have to say that the "physics" EA has hyped so much isn't there either. The physics of player on player impact is far superior in Madden 12 and so is the AI. I will give this one example from the dozens per game that I see. Playing with the 49ers I watched Aldon Smith make 3 sacks in one game that were totally "instant replay" versions of each other and they were total BS when it comes to the physics of game play. Smith was coming at the QB who was looking at the opposite side of the field on all 3 plays so what would you expect from a hard hitting linebacker coming in full speed on a QB totally unaware that he's about to be hit? A monster driving hit chest to chest to try to cause a fumble? That's what I expected to see and what I saw time and time again in Madden 12 but in Madden 13 when Smith impacted the QB it was like he hit a brick wall and stopped dead from full speed and instead had to grab the QB and twist him to the ground. Huh? Really? And this is suppose to be a physics engine? After playing enough games the real problems with the new engine are huge and glaring and go way beyond players flopping and flailing on the ground after the play is over.

09/27/12
I decided to try playing a game online to see if any of the online adjustments made so far have made the game better and no it has not. Every 2 or 3 plays I watch one of my defensive backs just take off toward the opposite sideline at a 90 angle (sometimes he goes behind the QB and sometimes he goes behind the MLB) with no regard what so ever for the play called and what is taking place on the field. I didn't even finish the first quarter before I turned it off and put Madden 12 back in. There is no sense in playing such a piece of garbage and there is no sense in such an obvious AI error still being there after more than a month. I'm guessing that the safeties still become statues in the middle of the field too when the other team is way behind allowing receivers to just run free behind them but I didn't play long enough to find out.
10/17/12
This is my last update because this was my last attempt at playing this crap. Update #3 from EA...losts of promises from EA but I guess it was just an attempt to keep sales up as word got out about how bad this crap is because if anything the game is worse than before. Do your self a favor and save your money. I wouldn't even buy this crap out of the $5 used game bin.
top_profane.title: DO NOT GET THIS-Last update after patch #3-DO NOT GET THIS CRAP!

top_profane.title: One Star

top_profane.text:
So I've been hearing about other reviews for this game and how it's been averaging fairly low (5-6 out of 10) with "professional" game reviewers. Remember, folks, the word "professional" just means that someone is paid to do something...not that they are necessarily good at it.

First and foremost, realize a title like WKC isn't hyped enough for the publisher to bribe video game review websites for a decent review (unlike Grand Theft Auto IV or Modern Warfare 2). Also...if you think IGN or Gamespot or any other review website isn't "bribed" in some way to ignore or make light of a highly hyped game's sucky spots then I want some of the kool-aid you're drinking so that I can ride home on the mothership with you.

With that being said, let's get down to the game.

The Good:

Graphics - The latest consoles have pretty much spoiled us. We expect greatness and beauty and this game delivers. The characters are very detailed and the environments are rich and colorful. I am still amazed at how well the latest games can capture the look of water. The devil is in the details and WKC delivers very well.

Sound - The voice acting is about par for a decent JRPG. There are some seriously corny parts but if you enjoy Japanese RPGs and anime then everything will mesh perfectly. The music is very lovely and flows right along with the game. It manages to fade into the background for a pleasant "white noise" effect as should the music in any RPG. Those of you reading this review that cut your teeth on the 8-bit classics of Zelda and Final Fantasy will most likely already have the ability to filter out repetitive gameplay music. Fortunately for WKC, the music is infinitely better than a tune that can be accurately demonstrated on a xylophone and I find myself listening to it frequently without wishing I could scoop my eardrums out and throw them down a garbage disposal.

Gameplay - Turn based RPG with a twist. I really enjoy building my own fighting combos from the base list of acquired moves. If a certain move becomes boring you are able to change it up a bit. Not to mention that if a certain enemy winds up really pissing you off that you can (minus banging your hands together to form a sword) transform into Voltron and beat the ever loving crap out of it. Transforming Leonard into the White Knight is great once he's fully transformed. Suddenly you're not so scary any more are you, you piece of troll arse!

The Bad:

With the exception of Chrono Trigger, EVERY game has at least one sucky spot in it somewhere...and WKC is no exception.

Graphics - The game is stunning. Seriously. However, I find myself wishing for a bit more detail on the monsters. Why am I fighting little evil beavers and venus flytraps on crack? That was seriously the most creative thing they could come up with? The trolls are cool looking as well as the Magi knights...but when I get up close they seem like they should be fighting Gumby. It's like every effort was made to avoid enemies with faces and detail. The plants are just a green sideways pac-man, the little evil beavers are wearing Klan masks, the troll is all nose, and the knights all have on helmets. I imagine this was to save time so that it could be spent on the main heroes and villians...but at the end of the day I feel like the hero should be wearing a big Jack-in-the-Box head. Also...what is with the "Avatar"? I custom made my avatar and spent a lot of time on him and when I'm done he looks like a damn nutcracker compared to Leonard. I even put a mole under one of his eyes and he STILL looks like friggen Pinnoccio! The only good thing about the custom character is that I can give him the same eyes that Cartman from South Park had when he was trying to fart out a satellite that aliens had stuffed up his anus.

Sound - There is one certain character that I already want to go back and lead into an ambush so that he dies. Why is it in EVERY game the character with the biggest ears (Batman excluded) has the most annoying voice EVER? The little rabbit creatures make me want to drop-kick baby otters. While running around the characters in your party will sometimes just spout out random crap. THE SAME RANDOM CRAP OVER AND OVER AND OVER. I would even be fine if they made fun of me, "Damn it...this guy keeps running us into every freaking enemy on the map!" or "Wrong way, a**h***!" No...I have to hear about "That dark knight...he was formidable." Or something along those lines. That's the ONLY good thing about the Avatar character...he has no voice of his own because he's supposed to be YOU. Ugh...

Gameplay - I hate multiplayer on any game. HATE it. If I wanted to play an online "RPG" I'd go play with the little foul mouthed jerks on WoW that haven't hit puberty yet. So...I make my Avatar and then he's introduced to the main character as "the new guy". He has NO voice of his own and just makes facial expressions and grunts. Basically...he's the RPG version of Beaker from the Muppets. He even makes the jaw drop face. I kept waiting for him to get electrocuted and for his eyeballs to light up. All we're missing is for the Swedish Chef to start throwing dead chickens around screaming "UM GESH DE BORK BORK!" That being said I cannot comment on the multiplayer (I'll leave that to other reviewers) but am thouroughly enjoying the single player now that I have enough other characters to stuff the stupid self-made avatar out of sight.

Future reference for game makers: SEPARATE THE MULTIPLAYER!!!! If I want to play multiplayer then let me make a multiplayer character or make the multiplayer character grow along with Leonard but keep him out of sight. The idea of the "Avatar" is completely freaking stupid. It's like I have this mute squire following me around waiting for me to tell him to shine my boots and drops his jaw straight down every time something dramatic happens. Remember the chancellor from Chrono Trigger? Yeah, the Avatar makes a face just like that but in today's graphics.

The battle elements have a few...quirks. The "Wait Meter" that is present in most turn-based RPGs is annoying as hell. It's a huge obtrusive circle that disappears if an enemy steps out of range so that you have to reset the damn thing every time it closes. Not to mention that you transform back into boring old Leonard if you're Voltron and kick the crap out of something and there are no more enemies around. What the hell is that? What if I wanted to run over to another troll a few screens away after I finish using up ALL of my Action Coins on becoming Voltron? I then have to go and get smacked in the face by KKK Beavers and green sideways Pac-Mans or score a bunch of hits to build back up my action coins.

Also...why the hell does it take so long to become The White Knight? I know Voltron took forever to transform but that was stupid too. If I were the badguy in Voltron I'd run up and start kicking the stupid right out of Voltron while his ass was trying to transform. Well...in THIS game the badguys figured the same thing. While I'm reciting the entire latin phrase found on the side of a pack of cigarettes and the bottom of three different brands of beer the badguys are beating the ever loving poo out of my face.

Also...for being an invention of the Ancients and having ultimate power the White Knight is slow as hell. I have to wind up like I'm getting ready to tee off on a lobbed baseball and then all of my moves look the same. Why the hell does Voltron have to be so damn slow? Grab that sword in two hands if you have to and CHOP THE BADGUY!!!

Leveling up is also nice...but I can't tell what the hell I'm going to unlock after I choose a skill. This is stupid, in my opinion. I should be able to see what the progress will be of learning a certain move and if I deem it as useless then I can move on to the next training topic.

I'll end here and say that, all-in-all, this is a great Japanese RPG. If you do not like JRPGs, however, you will not like this game...and just like the professional review sites you had no business playing this game in the first place.
top_profane.title: If you like JRPGs then WKC is your game!

top_profane.text:
I did a lot of research before buying this mouse about 9 months ago. I really liked the amount of features and customization you could do with it. But I also read a lot of reviews talking about how the WoW app is a glitchy piece of crap (it is), and that the mouse stops tracking after a few months (it did).

I haven't even had this 100+ dollar thing for more that a year and already the laser stops tracking either horizontally or vertically at random. It started about 3 months after I bought it (just beyond warranty range), it was pretty rare, maybe once or twice a week. Now it's every day, multiple times a day. The only way to get it to stop screwing up, is to beat the crap out of it. I generally don't like to have to beat the crap out of something I paid $120 for, just to keep it functioning.

When it WORKS, the design is odd but not un-intuitive. And the side-hat button, and various other buttons worked really great for an mmo class with a lot of moves to track (a hunter in WoW). Granted, as I said, the WoW mod that comes with it is useless because it constantly forgets how you set it up. However, once you get used to programming hotkeys, and macros yourself, it becomes very easy to be dependent on the many things this mouse can do. Which just makes it all the more sad that it's build quality is such crap.

I should have known this would be the case with a Mad Catz product from the many crap console controllers I had from them as a kid. It seems to be their hallmark: Innovative ideas, Crap execution.

I'll say crap one more time. Crap.

Edit: I've since upgraded to a Corsair m95 Vengeance. It's about half the price. And although the buttons are not quite as intuitive as the mmo7's. The optics are a DREAM. Smooth as butter and incredibly accurate. By comparison the mmo7's optics had ALWAYS been a little janky, I just didn't know any better since I had only just upgraded from a 10 year old 1st gen Microsoft laser mouse. (Which still works by the way, they made those first ones to LAST) Now that I have a proper frame of reference, I can honestly and truly say that Mad Catz is...crap.
top_profane.title: Nice, while it works.

top_profane.text:
So you just purchased Simcity! Congrats! Been waiting a long time right? Me too. Let's start playing this simulation. First thing we need to do is download. Amazon is pretty good with that. 50MBPS you say? Shouldn't take too long.
*not too long goes by
Great. We have the game downloaded. Press start! Crap. We need to download Origin. What's Origin you ask? Um, it's a contact delivery system EA uses. It's very much like Steam, only it doesn't work as well, oh and those rumors on account bans and accusations of spying. Yea, they are true.... Anyway...
Great we have Origin. Lets start. Oh crap. Origin updates. Only a few minutes...Done. Lets.. Crap. Damn. Yea just create an account. Super easy. Okay done right? Nice. Open Origin. Perfect. There is Simcity! click play. What the heck? Not released yet? Refresh. Refresh.Refresh. Refresh. Refresh. There we go. Click play! okay. Damn. We have to download more updates. 1%....5%...8%...13%...18%....23%...24%...25%...28%...33%...44%...49%...57%...62%...78%...80%...84%...89%.... Server is down? damn. Log out. okay? Log back in. There we go. 91%...94%...99%....99%...99%... done. Geez. This is sure taking a long time. But we got over the major hump. Now it's pure Simcity bliss. okay. Select a server! Wait what? Can't select one? huh. Log out of Origin. Log back in. Same issue? Log out again. Log back in? There we go. Let's see. Both west servers are full. Try East 2. Perfect. It's not busy. Connecting.....Connecting. Server timeout. Damn. Uh try East 1. Connecting...Connecting... perfect! we are in. Welcome {your user name here). okay so there is a small tutorial. Big deal Let's just rush through it so we can build our own damn city right? okay. Click, Click, Click, wait, wait,Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, wait, wait, wait, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Almost done! Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, done. Now lets start our own city! WOOHOO! Let's do private. It's a damn single player game anyway right? that Titan Forge looks cool. Let's pick that city on the far east side. Good resources. Let's do it. Click create! Server issues? Can't create? What the hell? Go back to the main menu. WHAT THE HELL! server is down completely. UGH! Log out of Origin. Log back in! Dammit! pick East 1 this time. It's available. Welcome (your user name here). okay here we go. WHAT IN THE WORLD IS WRONG WITH THIS GAME?! Have to do the tutorial again? WTF?! Click, Click, Click, wait, wait,Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, wait, wait, wait, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Almost done! Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, Click, done. Finally. okay pirvate, Titan forge, sandbox mode, create. FINALLY A GAME. Let's get building. Build, build, build. Dude. Looking good. Add a police station here! And lets put in a mayor mansion here to increase land value! DUDE. Start a city over there for mining and your Sims can go work over there. Yeah! Perfect. Concentrate on mining on this city. Industrial too. What the heck? More server issues? CRAP! it kicked us out. Go back to your current games. Click on the first city. Make sure everything is oka.... WHAT THE HELL. Restore? or Abandon? Where the heck is my city? It's lost? Fine. Let's rebuild it. We can make it better right? Nice looking good. GAHHH! More server issues? Log out. Log back in. Server east 2. 30 minute waiting period! NOOOO! East 1! OUR CITY ISN"T THERE. THIS GAME IS CRAP! ORIGIN, DRM, S***TY SERVERS, NO PRE-DOWNLOAD, LOST CITY'S?

Do yourself a favor. DO NOT BUY THIS GAME. I MADE THE MISTAKE. I've only played for an hour and the city was lost. Now I can't even connect. EA and Origin sucks.
top_profane.title: I can help you set up Simcity!

top_profane.text:
The below review I posted months ago.

The game has been updated a couple times since then. They added new weapon variants, some new characters, and weapons.

To sum it up. This game is still terrible.

Because it's such a terribly buggy game that freezes often in multiplayer especially against a specific enemy type called the kett and when one enemy called the fiend is in play. This happens so often most players know about this issue. An issue that I can guarantee will never be fixed. As I said, the buggy nature of this game resulted in my being unable to load the game. I only put about 50+ hours into the boring and terrible single-player, but I figured I may still force myself to play the terrible multiplayer. So I deleted and redownloaded the game. Now I reserved this game (big mistake) with some of the bonus material. After redownloading the game, this "bonus" material was no longer available. As a result, all my previous saves were nullified and would not load. So, this meant I had to start the single player again. I got about 15 hours in until I couldn't take it anymore. The cheesy lines, the bad graphics, the terrible gameplay, the boring exploration, the clumsy six-wheeled car/tank thing.

But I kept playing the multiplayer (MP) and it is just awful. From basic game mechanics to gameplay to reliability to playability, the game is just awful. I often find myself repeating the same thing out loud everytime I play: "I HATE THIS F------ING GAME!" I say this phrase so often I then constantly ask myself why I keep playing it. The only answer I can think of is that I hope it might get better when I level up this character or this gun. But deep down I know that isn't going to happen. The game is just so terrible at it's root, nothing will make it better. It's like a bad vegan burger patty I once had. It's supposed to taste as good as a real burger but it didn't. No amount of cheese, condiment, or spices I added would make this taste better:
- weapons are weak
- aiming is terrible
- powers are weak/unreliable
- movement is laggy, clumsy, slow
- there is built in lag, meaning after everything you do: use a power, fire a weapon, dash, there is a pause before you can do anything else
- terrible cover system
- ledge grabbing is overused, forced and clumsy
- enemy AI is unbalanced and overpowered compared to playable characters

Consider this: these "geniuses" behind andromeda had to release patches that weakened enemies and increased weapon damage. But just to be the jerks that they are, the reduced damage or some feature of some of the favored weapons and increased damage of some of the terrible weapons. Remember the vegan burger? Terrible is still terrible.

They came up with a variant to all the weapons called a "siphon" that give you back health based on the damage your weapon causes. Specifically a small fraction (around 3%) of the damage you cause returns as health. At least that's what the description says. It doesn't work that way though. I noticed in ME3 that bioware doesn't know how math works. You might pick a power perk that increases base damage by 50% but in reality it only increases it by 30%. Use a siphon weapon on weaker enemies and you get zero to nearly zero health back. It doesn't matter if you kill several base enemies if your health is low, your still screwed and will die is an enemy even looks at you in the wrong way. Use that same siphon weapon on a more powerful enemy and you get significantly more health back. Mind you significantly more than zero isn't a large amount. This makes no sense. The damage a weapon outputs is the same regardless of you you shoot. But that's just how andromeda rolls. It's asinine. It pushes agendas. It takes the joy from gameplay by making things weak, clumsy, slow, lagged, corny, lame, and boring..... all by design.

These people surely never played ME3 multiplayer and if they did, they must have been terrible at it. They took everything that made ME3 multiplayer great and ruined it. Examples:
- Great weapons that were well balanced and had character (i.e. Javelin sniper, M99 Saber, Particle Rifle, Cerberus Harrier) are non-existent in this game or there is a version of the weapon in andromeda that just terrible.
- Great characters that were well balanced and had character... same description as above
- cover grab (grab someone in cover and beat instakill them)
- Cobra missiles (one shot kill a group of enemies no questions asked)

Yet they took things that were hated in ME3 and forced their use in andromeda
- automated cover system
- clumsy useless auto targeting system
- very long load times
- the way the game lags when a new player joins the lobby mid-game (Yes, these "genuises" actually made this happen in every game you play, even if you are alone in a private lobby, the game will freeze and lag for a moment early in round one. Not just at the beginning, no. It happens inconveniently after you've already been engaged by the enemy).
- Some of the least liked weapons in ME3 have for some reason found their way into andromeda. Whereas the aforementioned beloved weapons are no where to be found.

Stupid, just plain absolutely stupid game design. I have a deep loathing for the people behind this game. I tried to get into it. I forced myself to play it. I have put in now hours, but dozen and dozens of days worth of time into this game and just find myself hating it more and more.

Most of my friends have either stopped playing this game or gone back to ME3.

If I start a lobby, I would end up sitting for 10, 15, 20 minutes with no one joining. If I start a game on my own, I could potentially go through the entire mission solo. If you paid attention to anything of what I've been saying, you can imagine how miserable and frustrating playing this game solo can be. If I try to join another lobby, it is a guarantee that I will load into the match after the enemies in the first wave have already spawned and I will probably be killed before even loading in. I will likely be paired with rookie players that are just terrible, or in some rare cases, with the dozen or two andromeda players that only do three things in life: eat, sleep, play andromeda. I've spoken with some of these guys, and they have all their weapons and characters maxed out. They are not all good players, in fact I am better than most of them. Fan boys will say I hate thing game because I'm not good at it. Well, I have been ranked in the top 30 since the game came out. In 90% of the games I play, I am ranked #1 in points. I've had many players compliment me on how well I play or hate on me for how well I play. That doesn't matter. I may be good at the game. But I still hate it. I still find it frustrating and asinine.

If there was an video game award for most disappointing sequel to a hit game, the maker of andromeda should receive this award along with a termination letter preventing them from ever working on video games again.
______________________________________________________________________________________________________________

There are plenty of the reviews about the single-player which boils down to this: It's not horrible, but far from good.

With that being said, this review is a hard look at the multi-player of ME:A. For my background. I'm an older gamer. I've played the original Mass Effect, ME2, and enjoyed the story line when I was younger. Years later though, I find that games I loved 10 or 15 years ago are boring and tedious now. I just don't have the patience for them as I did before. But multiplayer I love to play. Fast paced and action packed and of course very competitive. I never gave ME3's mp a second thought until I realized it can have an impact on the controversial ME3 ending. So I started playing it, and fell in love with it. I stopped playing most other games. I was enthralled with ME3 mp. Years of playing and I still learned new strategies and skills that I wasn't aware of before. It was a thoroughly enjoyable, yet far from perfect mp game. It seemed clear that ME3's MP was an after thought that didn't get a great deal of focus until EA or Bioware or whoever started to realize how many gamers were still playing the MP. So, it is for this reason mainly that I sunk $60+ hard earned, money I really couldn't afford, into Mass Effect Andromeda only for it to become an infamously terrible game according to many reviews. Worse, I now see it for sale at nearly half the reserved price. I actually wasn't going to reserve the game but many online gamer friends I met as a result of ME3's mp urged me to get Andromeda so we can all play together. Incidentally, most of those friends no longer play Andromeda. So, if you enjoyed ME3's mp here is a list I compiled of why ME:A mp is a worse comparison.

10 Things I HATE about Mass Effect: Andromeda Multiplayer (btw I LOVED ME:3 MP)

1. Weak arse weapons. Yes there are some ultra-rares that do moderate to good damage.
Though a majority of the weapons are truly garbage. You can get around this by using power based characters.
But for example, an UR weapon from ME:3 that I loved, the M99 Saber which was pretty good even at level 1, you won't find in Andormeda MP. I could, with the AR damage mod and a scope, snipe and one-shot head-shot kill most weak to moderate enemies.
In this shite game, the Black Widow sniper rifle often fails to kill a base enemy on silver! There's a sniper called the
isharey that has something like with 2000 damage in a single shot. I used this sniper with an infiltrator character thus giving it near
100%+ damage when in cloak. I've head-shot base enemies in gold and it failed to kill the enemy. It's truly ridiculous how
weak these weapons are. They even added a patch that increased weapon damage and weakened enemies because the balance was so bad upon release. This helped with game play somewhat but like everything else in the game, it's still lacking.

2. I seriously feel like there were people developing or designing this game that are truly arseholes who just wanted
to ruin the fun or the joy in the game with some asinine idiotic ideas that were unfortunately implemented in this game.
Two of which are the ledge grabbing BS and walking. Yes, for reasons that are beyond my comprehension, perhaps to immitate this stupid design from other MP games, but now you can walk in ME andromeda. Yes, because being able to walk as opposed to run was something
I'm sure gamers from ME3 were hoping for. You can press down on the directional control stick to run, but why should that even be an option?! Furthermore, running is so slow in this game to the point of frustration. The jet pack is an okay idea although somewhat gimmicky. I was worried when I heard they were adding this feature that they would add some gimmick like ledge grabs, just to push their obnoxious agendas (and that is what ruined this game to me,
the way they force agenda's into the game whether it be game mechanics or political). Sure enough, in the single player,
you will find yourself becoming friggen mario from mario brothers hopping and jumping here and there to get to a stupid objective to activate this console to go to this area to activate another console to go back to that first area to re-activate another console, hippity hoppity jumpity whoo. I dare say it's worse that mario brothers.
I can barely tolerate the constant unnecessary jumping. But what "grinds my gears" is this stupid idea of placing literally
every jump point, every ledge, everything just out of reach of being able to be jumped ON and instead you have to rely
on the stupid buggy game mechanics to allow your character to grab the ledge and pull you up onto the surface of whatever
it was you were trying to jump on. So if it's a multiplayer game, and you need to jump onto the upper floor of an area to get away
from enemies shooting at you or to get to an objective or downed teammate, there are times where no matter how much or hard you mash the controller button, you seem to be jumping just out of reach or your character fails to grab the ledge. But there is another factor that
makes things worse but I will get to that in number 3. Mass effect fan boys will deny this is a problem but I've often seen
players try again and again to jump onto a ledge area that they or I have been able to "jump" (and by jump I mean hope the
stupid game mechanic allows you to grab a ledge and pull you up) onto before. Whichever shiteheads thought it would be a good idea
to add WALKING and LEDGE GRABBING I hope are fired from their job.

3. Remember in ME3 how you would be running away from a banshee chasing your or your trying to get to a downed teammate and your
character kept slapping up against the wall or any object you come close to trying to take cover. The game mechanics
in ME3 were clumsy, yet the game was still fairly enjoyable. But the cover system mechanic was frustrating. So much so, that I eventually learned to play ME3 without using much cover. Instead I would stand near an obstacle and not push the button to take cover. If I need to take cover, I'll just stand behind a wall or corner. My shields will come back up in a couple seconds
as well as my powers and I'll be good to go. Well, some arse decided to create an automatic cover system. See? Like I said,
they push their agenda. Now ANYTIME you approach ANYTHING your character will try to take cover behind it. Trying to jump to
a higher area, well you will often find yourself fighting with the game mechanics trying to make you take cover on that wall you are
trying to jump over. I cannot express to you how frustrating it is to try and get away from a group of enemies coming after you as you try to escape by jumping up over a wall with cover only to find for some reason you can't get
high enough, even though you've done it several times before in the same damned mission, whilst watching your character idiotically
taking cover against the wall in front of you, and the enemies are shooting you from behind. I've even had my character try and take cover against an enemy! The jumping is completely random. Sometimes you get high enough, sometimes you won't. There's one map in particular where sometimes I can jump completely over a wall with cover and other times I can only jump high enough for the stupid computer to let me grab the ledge and pull myself over, then there's times where I can't even jump high enough to do that. It's one hard press of the button and yet I can get three different outcomes! I hate the cover sytem! There's a map called "Icebreaker" with ice/snow on the ground. The ground is therefore very irregular in some placed. As a result, your character will idiotically try to take cover from the small dips in the ground. Since you obviously can't take cover behind a few inches of ice, your character will hesitate and you have to jump over the irregularity to avoid the stupid game mechanics getting confused trying to take cover.

But it gets worse! In ME3, like I said, it doesn't take long for your shields to recharge. But once again the arseholes strike back with
their shite agendas, because in this game if you want your shields to recharge almost like it did in ME3, you HAVE to take cover. If you don't,
it could take as much as 5 seconds or more for your shields to START to recharge, and even then it does it slowly at first and builds up in speed
right around the time it's fully charged. Five seconds is a lifetime doing a gold match surrounded by enemies, whilst having to jam down on the controller stick so you can sprint and pushing the stick forward for you to go. I've had my xbox one for years. Played multiple games on the xbox one (including ME3!!!) and never had the problem of my controller sticking. Now it does. The stick has been damaged because out of frustration because the characters in this game are so damned SLOW!
Even with sprint! You'll find players constantly spamming the jet pack so they can slide a few feet forward more quickly. But just like ME3's few characters that had the ability to dash forward, backward, or to the sides, there is still a pause before you can do it again. Thus, a player just running behind you, can nearly keep up despite the dash. So it's just a futile attempt to use the jet pack to move further. It's just too damned slow. Why is this a problem. Well if you are getting shot at by the enemy, you will die before you can get away or find cover. It's incredibly frustrating.

4. Lag is still an issue. With the most recent patch, you can see the connection strength of the host but I find that baffling. I have my xbox one directly connected. I have connection speeds as fast as 80+ mbps with uploads as high as 20mpbs and pings as low as 15ms. YET, if I start a lobby, it will take FOREVER to find any players, but if I go to a quick search I can find a team in a second. In ME3 I rarely had that problem. I can host all day and no one really complained of lag issues.
But in this game, even if I find a host with a strong connection, I often experience lag. Sometimes it's simply game breaking, other times it just damned annoying. You would think that when adding things like jet packs, sprinting, faster paced combat, etc. this might give the developers pause to think "should we add dedicated servers and if not, we should at least consider this added stuff will this cause frame rate drops,
or other issues for non-hosting players?" No of course not. That's not what arseholes do.

5. The characters in multiplayer look ridiculous and will say some of the most cheesiest things like: "Skidaddle!", "coolio", "gracias" (in the most non-spanish accent possible), or "friendly neighborhood hydra". Also, most characters seem to have a complete lack of understanding about how their shields work or just have a plain disdain for their shields. Then there's the alcoholic agenda "Next rounds on me" or "drinks or on me". Some of them look like someone decided to take some sporting equipment armor and wear that along with a motorcycle helmet,
a plastic looking gun with some "wear" on the paint scheme, some denim jeans or leather to go into battle. Seriously it's kind of laughable. And to push their social agenda, there HAS to be an equal or greater number of female characters. So get ready to hear some deep sounding krogan females voices. I don't even consider myself a misogynist. I remember ME3 having a fair amount of female characters
most of which were my favorite (i.e. alliance infiltrator, N7 Fury) but in this game the female additions feel forced. To make matters worse, you have to unlock cards via purchase of money or credits, that work completely through chance to unlock additional skill points to fully upgrade your character. This doesn't take too long but for the ultra rare characters, this could take months if not years
depending how much time (or money) you have to invest in playing this game and just plain luck. What they should have done is require the player to reset the level and level up certain characters a number of times
to eventually unlock their full potential. In doing so, you kinda have to earn the fully unlocked version of that character as well as learn how to play that character well. But nope, the card lottery is what they want.
And I know why! I know why the multiplayer started out so hard, why the guns were so weak, why the gameplay is kinda awful. They hoped that you will give in to your frustrations and just start spending your money to buy cards to get better
weapons and characters. I refuse! In ME3 they used this lottery system as well. It can get frustrating at times, but there were so many great guns and characters, it was worth the wait and effort. This system can be defined as a variable-positive reinforcer. It's kind of how a manipulative significant other who generally was a terrible boyfriend/girlfriend, but you stay with them due to those seemingly random intermittent periods of good times in the relationship. It's part of the behaviorists psychology of behavior reinforcement and why gambling can be such a strong addiction for some.

6. Aiming. Remember there were certain enemies in ME3 that were just damned annoying and invoked your hatred because they were so damned hard to target and moved so fast, like the nemesis and phantom from cerberus? Well, the arseholes from ME andromeda must have thought you loved those characters because now most of the enemies run, dodge, sprint, duck, shuffle, hop and do all kinds of moves to avoid getting hit by your powers and YES even your weapon.

I'm not kidding. I've played this game and fired a sniper rifle at an enemy facing away from me, only to see that enemy hop to the side just as I fired. The AI is that damned annoying. If they dodge weapon fire like this,
how many times do you think they dodge powers like throw and incinerate which still do the stupid arc and hit walls, ceilings, etc. rather than the enemy. If this wasn't bad enough, aiming has become extremely frustrating.
Especially if you are not hosting.

7. Certain weapon types, powers, and equipment from ME3 are no where to be found in Andromeda which I just hate. For example, the only weapon that comes close to the M99 I so loved from ME3 is a sniper called the vanquisher. Unfortunately,
another annoying thing about this game are the slow reload times. That's another issue for number 8. Most of the assault rifles in this game are absolutely terrible. Truly like I said most of the guns are. But powers like the recon mine are
nowhere to be found, grenade mechanics seem to defy logical physics and are thrown like they are extra bouncy fluffy nerf balls or land no where near where you intended. Worse, they added "targeting assist" with grenades that are hilariously unreliable. I've thrown sticky grenades at a hydra (one of the largest enemies) and watched as the grenade arc will curve left or right completely missing the enemy, even when the enemy is standing still. But if I throw the grenade at an enemy standing near or partially behind cover, the grenade will land on that cover, thus causing no damage. The one armor equipment I used with nearly every character in ME3 was the Geth scanner (which allows you to see enemies highlighted even behind walls) there's nothing like that here. Oh, they can most certainly create such a mod since in every mission when you get to the last three enemies they are highlighted or the fact that they have one character that uses this as a power. But an equipment mod that does this? Nope. I'm certain if you polled top players from ME3 Multiplayer and asked if they like this scanning mod, they would say yes.
There are few characters that have reliable crowd control powers other than the flamethrower that a couple characters have. Perhaps you can spam a bunch of grenades but like everything else, those can be weak as well.

8. Apparently people must have complained about how fast reloading weapons were was in ME3 and the arsholes wanted to remedy that. In ME3 you can almost cut the obnoxious, lazy, casual reload animation. Not in this game. Sometimes, even when you wait for the animation to finalize and you move, jump, use cover, use a power, or get blown by a gentle breeze, you have to friggen reload again. Sometimes even after patiently waiting to reload and waiting a little bit longer just in case of lag, after firing you weapon, your character will automatically start reloading again, even though you haven't emptied your clip!

9. The unusual randomness of everything makes me hate this game further. I can use a gun to headshot a base character in this game and it won't kill them leaving some health visible. In the same mission, using the same gun, on another base character, that shot might kill him.
Yet again, same gun, same base character, same mission, I can headshot and the entire health bar is gone, yet the character is still running around alive, even when I'm host. Most would instantly blame lag, but I've seen this happen as I'm hosting the game!

Powers will sometimes kill an enemy, sometimes they wont. Sometimes the power will work, sometimes it won't. Sometimes you set up a combo explosion, sometimes it just doesn't happen. There's a character called the asari sentinal that has a biotic shield that deflects enemy fire and some powers. If you have good timing,
you can bring up that shield and deflect up to 500% damage back at the enemy (of course this only scratches most enemies since they are faster, stronger, can jump higher, and move faster than most of the MP characters you can use). But after pressing the button the shield will go up then immediately go back down. Since I put myself out there depending on my shield to deflect weapon fire or a power, how frustrating is it you think to have that power start then just as immediately come off and I get hit or die? Another character, the human kinetistic has the power to
pull an enemy and throw them great distances. Wellllll LOL, sometimes the enemy gets pulled. Sometimes they don't. Sometimes when pull actually works as it should, throw doesn't work. Sometimes when pull properly works and throw properly works I can only throw the characters a few feet.
Sometimes! Sometimes when pull properly works, and throw properly works, and I can actually throw them seemingly off the map, the bastards come back and are shooting me again! Yes, even after throwing them seeming 1000 meters off the map and down a cliff, they somehow comeback!
I've seen other complain about this on other forums. ME3 was certainly not bug free and had it's issues but not as bad as this game.

10. The never powerful cobra RPG. In ME3 this was the GODSMACK of weapons. When you needed to clear every MOFO in the room no questions asked you can almost always rely on the cobra missile. Well guess what the andromeda arseholes have done?! In this game, I've used a cobra rpg on a atlas (Erm I mean Hydra)
only to see half it's armer health go down. Then a teammate will use their cobra rpg and the damned enemy is still alive with some health still there! It would take a third RPG to finish the thing off. It doesn't matter if you hit the "weak point" because like everything in this
damned game, everything is so buggy and random. I once used 5 cobra missiles to kill a fiend. I remember that was one of my most frustrating games because I lost the lead in point due to being instakilled by a fiend in wave 3 because none of my teammates were doing the mission except myself. Then every consecutive wave after
I was constantly stalked by every fiend that spawned. Despite using a fully upgraded character, gold level incindiary ammo, and a fully leveled rare smg, it took nearly a minute to kill one fiend. Then, literally as soon as I killed that one, another had spawned and charged me nearly picking me up for another instakill. I fired an rpg
it only took down a third of the health. I ran away to get enough distance to "aim" as best as can be done in this shit game, and hit the fiend in the face (weakspot) and it was STILL alive with a half of it's health. This was the extraction wave and the fiend was now sorrounded by enemies shooting at me, so I used another rpg at the floor
near the fiend and it failed to kill most of the enemies, even the base level enemies, and the fiend was STILL alive. So I used my fourth rpg and it looked like the fiend health was now completely gone but NOPE, it was still alive with what looked like an empty health bar and charging at me again. I said "screw it" and used my final rpg
to kill it. I mean, WTF?!?!? It was a gold match, but the hydra example I gave above was a silver match! Even if you factor in lag, the other teammate the took the second rpg shot at the hyrda was host! It two of my rpg's including his as the second shot to kill a hydra on silver?! And yes, I know there will be an andromeda fan boy who will announce
"I always kill a fiend with just one cobra rpg on gold" yeah well good for you, because I've rarely seen it happen when I do it or another teammate tries.

I continue to play this game because I've been in love with Mass Effect from the very first game and have spent countless hours playing ME3 multiplayer and had gotten damned good at it. Andromeda's multiplayer isn't terrible, but it is damned frustrating and annoying. It's really a disappointment and I spent my hard earned money on this game
and feel as though I should try to get something out of it, but damn. I just had to vent about the frustrations I have with this game.
top_profane.title: A Review for the Lover of ME3's Multiplayer

top_profane.text:
With the 18 Wheels of Steel franchise, I was looking forward to this new addition from Valusoft. Weeks after it was announced, I was happy. Unfortunately, it was short lived. I thought this was one game on a DVD and something new. Half right and half wrong.

Was this something new? Yes
Is it one whole game? No
Did the franchise take a step back? Yes (18 WoS: Convoy was a step back)

Adding a new twist to the 18 Wheels of Steel franchise, is the opporutinty to be the boss of a bus company. You also get the opportunity to own your own garbage collecting company. You even get to own your own trucking company. Here's the twist.....It is 3 seperate games. You get 18 Wheels of Steel: American Long Haul, Big City Rigs: Bus Driver ( not to be confused with Bus Driver by Merscom) and Big City Rigs: Garbage Truck Driver.

Bus Driver is extremely buggy. cars will come out of no where and "land" on your bus. You would be stuck and have to quit the mission. You get different buses, but they pretty much have the same bells and whistles. The cities that you drive through, are more cardboard than alive. Picture the cities in American Long Haul, then shrink them by half, and finally, lower the graphic quality by three-quarters. Very Disappointing. To earn money, you charge a price to enter the bus, and drive along the route, shown on the map that is visible. The green dots show where you have to pick up passengers. A few times, I honked the horn and a car out of no where, lands on my bus and I'm stuck. To put it mildly, Bus Driver is very buggy. It gets boring after some time also. To make money you have to run the same bus line several times before you can purchase a new bus in your fleet. The tours which you can also run, are buggy also. I was doing one tour and when I stopped at a location that was showing on the map as a dot, nothing happened. I drove a round a few times and even passed by the marker several times; nothing. Then time ran out. Again, very frustrating. The only thing less frustrating is that the traffic lights are slightly longer (when green) than in American Long Haul and Haulin.

Big City Rigs: Garbage Truck Driver doesn't have the bug problem as Bus Driver but the graphics are pretty much the same; bland. In Garbage Truck Driver, again, you own your own company and get to buy a garbage truck that is needed for the "morning run" (basically substitute the game, Bus Driver's passenger bus line runs for garbage cans). You buy trucks to go along with the mission that requires a specific garbage or recyclng truck. The cool thing is that you have a man or men, jump off the truck, go to the garbage, and bring it and empty it into the truck. The animation stops at when you have the garbage pail or garbage reach the truck. You don't see the animation of the garbage into the truck. You just see the garbage being thrown or emptied in. No hydraulics pushing the garbage, or showing if your truck is full. You don't even empty the truck at the end of the mission. These are a few of my gripes, but then again, I am a realism nut, so others will have a difference in a opinion. Same thing happens in this game also: you have to do multiple runs to earn money and then buy other trucks.

For 18 Wheels of Steel: American Long Haul, see my review of it on the 18 Wheel of Steel: American Long Haul product page here on Amazon. I got this even though I have ALH because of the special $[...] price. Basically, you get 3 games for $[...]. Instead of buying Bus Driver and Garbage Truck Driver for $[...] each (digital download), I got them both for $[...] (or less than that actually).

The police won't pull you over in Garbage Truck Driver and Bus Driver. You will be penalized in your bank account. Basically, it seems like both Garbage Truck Driver and Bus Driver were stripped down versions of American Long Haul concerning graphics and trucks were replaced with Buses and Garbage Trucks. The driving area is not as big as American Long Haul either.

Be warned: Bus Driver is buggy. Garbage Truck Driver will be somewhat disappointing and not hold your attention for long. American Long Haul is great, if you don't have Haulin. If you have 18WoS: Haulin, American Long Haul, is basically, the same thing.

I gave this 3 stars because the true rating in my honest opinion would be a 2.5 stars. Both Garbage Truck Driver and Bus Driver are new while American Long Haul has been out for a year. Bus Driver is very buggy and when the bugs aren't hurting the game, it gets boring. Garbage Truck Driver isn't as buggy but the same thing happens.....it just doesn't keep you in the game long enough.

For American Long Haul in my product review, I would love to see American Long Haul get an overhaul. This would include lively cities, more routes (secondary streets and highways), and to be able to get out of your truck. I'd love to get out of my truck and pump the gas or go to a truck wash. It would be nice to "go home" in the game to rest or actually go into a motel/hotel to rest. I would also love to be able to have workers load or unload the merchandise or products that I am hauling. How cool would it be, when you haul the new cars to the dealership and actually drive the cars off your trailer and onto the lot as well as giving the bill to the dealership. It would be amazing to have time off and go "home" and drive your car around to get food or you can use the time at home to pay bills. If it proves to be difficult to put real-life cities, then put fictional cities. But we have to remember that Valusoft tries to keep costs down ($[...] and under). Hopefully another company can go above and beyond. If ALH was overhauled, improved, and given more content, I wouldn't mind paying $[...] or even $[...]; if it's worth it.

*update*
I realized that 3 stars was very generous. I wish I can change it to 2 stars. Bus Driver and Garbage Truck Driver are a complete mess. If you have a passing interest in driving a bus, Bus Driver by Merscom is better. I'm still waiting for RignRoll.

*I'm running this on Vista Home Premium 64bit OS* (Intel i7 920 built by me)<---see it in my Listmania section
top_profane.title: Disappointing

top_profane.text:
I actually had to stop playing and write this review because I'm so frustrated with this crap. Sure, I expected a learning curve since I'm coming from the previous version of Rocksmith. But holy crap this is awful! Here are complaints after about two hours of playing with it:
From the time I click on the icon until I can actually play something is about six minutes (yes, my computer more than meets system requirements).
Getting from one menu to another is a terrible chore. It takes FOREVER just to get from one menu to another. Every time I try to go from one song to another or from a song to training or to whatever, I have to keep hitting escape over and over until the software decides to respond.
The training is choppy at best and I just want the voice to shut the hell up so I can practice something but it keeps talking .Really, how long does it take to explain what the hell a chord is.
The thing locked up on me four times so far. I had to turn the computer off because Rocksmith 2014 wouldn't let task manager on the top.
Volume is lower too. I have my speakers jacked and the volume seems about half that of the previous rocksmith (same PC).
Latency is much worse. I have to hit notes really early to have them count.
Did I mention that it locks up and then won't let task manager pop to the front so I actually had to power down to close this freaking software?
What the hell is this Uplay thing? Does that take the place of Steam? Now another password and user name to remember.
Oh, if I want to import songs from the previous version of Rocksmith, I HAVE TO PAY FOR THE UTILITY THAT ALLOWS ME TO DO IT!!!!!
I paid (what I consider to be) a lot of money for this piece of crap, so I'll continue to give it a chance, But for any one considering buying this, I would recommend staying far the heck away from this. Maybe Rocksmith 2016 will be better. But for now, I'm just in awe of the software developers skill in coming up with a game that annoys me to the point that I just want to throw my computer across the room. Really, I just can't express how much I hate this. Seems like it was rushed out the door to meet a deadline or something. That or the software guys are pissed off at humanity and are taking it out through this game.

One Week Update:
Ok, went from one star to three. I got to the point where I just decided to re-install the game, this helped a lot with the glitchiness. However the lag, latency, and sound were still terrible. Thanks to some fine, upstanding, and very savy users out there, I was able to tweek the audio settings on the computer and on the .ini file in the rocksmith directory. So I've been able to play at a tolerable level for a couple of days now. The only problems I still have is 1. I still think it's crap that I have to pay to port the songs from Rocksmith to 2014. 2. Video playback sucks. Like on the hammer on/pull off tutorial where they show a woman playing the guitar. I'm pretty sure I can fix this, but for now, it's not getting in my way. 3. The games are kinda cool but some are lacking good instructions. Like with the scales game, I feel pretty stupid that I couldn't figure that one out. And the chord zombie game, I like that but the example they give you was hard for me to read. If you don't have the colors to string association memorized (or if you're colorblind like me). 4. Where is colorblind mode? Probably somewhere obvious, but I've been spending so much time on other things I sort of just let it go for now. 5. I have to hit escape like eight times to exit the game. I really wish that there was an exit button on most screens. 6. Lag. I have to pluck the strings really early and I've been getting killed because all my notes are late. Apparently there's a workaround for this, I'll try it tonight. 7. Scoring. It will only show your high score in percentage form. Like if I get 50% and then the next time I get 40%, it still shows 50%. Not a big deal, but bugs the crap out of me. I want to know exactly what my score is every time I play.

Things I'm liking:
Don't have to tune so much
More games (some are pretty cool)
Fretboard seem more perpendicular to the eyes (if that makes any sense) so it's much easier to see what fret I'm supposed to be on.
Menu system is way better than RS. Still think it's a pain to exit the game but it is an improvement.

Overall:
I'm almost glad I bought it. Still pissed about the initial setup issues I had (have). I'm not a kid with a ton of time to play around so anything that gets in my way is taking time off of my life here.
top_profane.title: Sucked setting it up. But now I love it.

top_profane.text:
1 Star? Believe me, I am not a huge fan of 1 star or 5 star reviews. It would be REALLY hard to be that good or that bad. But you know what? I'm so sick and tired of this bigtime corporation (EA) ruining everything that they touch. Ever since Ultima Online, they just buy their way into things and ruin them over and over again. Let me tell you why this game sucks. There are more than a few reasons, but I'll put like 15 reasons in each category. (PC VERSION)

1- ACCOUNT creation mishaps. I went through everything bad that can happen to someone when they are creating an account for a game. EA Account, Merge with Origin account, ok sounds easy enough even though I shouldn't need this origin crap to begin with. But sure, I'll MEET YOUR DEMANDS. I merged accounts after having "e-mail already found" types of crap happening, and other stupid errors. I am not a genius, but after using computers for 15+ years I know how to make an account, and this site sucked for it. I installed origin crap, I installed the game. Finally, let's play this thing.

2- *double-clicks BF3 Icon* waits. waits. (my computer is pretty decent)ok, FIREFOX OPENS. WHAT? OK INSTALL PLUGINS... WHAT? oh my GOD... ok calm down. I install the plugins necessary to let EA take over my web browser. I'VE MET YOUR DEMANDS, TAKE MY COMPUTER! now let me try your game. Ok after crashing a few times before even getting to see the game yet, BOOM...I notice some battlelog crap open in my bottom right windows thing. WOAH, wait a minute, I need 4 (no exaggeration) programs working "TOGETHER" to run a FREAKING GAME. That's FOUR PROGRAMS. Origin, Firefox, Battlelog, BF3.exe. oh and if you count the plugin open in firefox, that's 5. The game launches through your webrowser on some crappy EA webpage for the game. I really can't take it... but it gets better.

3- Ok cool, I don't have a solid state drive, but after loading the game up for 2 minutes, (Skyrim loads in 10 seconds or less)I finally get to play. Ok, adjusting graphical settings... Well, my computer can only handle medium or so... not EA's fault there. READY to play. I go in the game. I'm getting 50 fps(nice) and the first thing I notice is my crosshair dissapears. NOT HARDCORE MODE, no. It just dissapears. Ok, must be my gun. Switch guns and it's back. Switch back and it's there... OK. Walk around a corner, ITS GONE. WOWOWOWOWOW, I HATE THIS GAME.

4- It's clunky in general. Pretty much every menu in the browser and in the game are clumsy and take getting use to. Not a HUGE minus because many games have this problem. But it's a Cherry on top to why this game sucks. I'm sick of typing now so I'll end with this.

EA is the single most crippling thing for video games this day and age. They got into the market at the right time, lots of people playing video games these days. They have their hand in almost everything and they are SOLELY out for money. They will water down the integrity of products to no end. They have deadlines, demands, and proprietary crap all over their name. I can't stand them, and until you give all of their crap 1 star, when it deserves it... they will continue to produce garbage.

I don't know much about DICE, so I won't diss them. But that EA/ORIGIN thing is what made this game the worst for me.

4GB patch came out already... THATS HOW BROKEN IT WAS/IS. STOP BUYING THESE REHASHED WARGAMES AND FEEDING THAT CORPORATE GARBAGE MACHINE. I don't usually rage, but I am genuinely raging right now. I plan to play SWTOR, and I'm SOOO afraid that EA will ruin it.
top_profane.title: I'm not going to be nice anymore.  This company should be ashamed of themselves.

top_profane.text:
I first bought this game in a Hastings book store a long time ago and I've been a Mk fan for quit some time, so I went ahead and bought it thinking "This is great! I've got MK trilogy that has all of my favorite characters in it. And Midway makes really great games.(LOL) And I managed to find it in a five dollar bin. This is truly a lucky day for me."
Until I played it....

Then as time went on, I kept it in my basement, and then one day I decided to bring it out because I hadn't played it in a while and I forgotten all of the moves. So I went to the options menu and put it on a pansy level so I can get the feel for it again. Then I remembered why I put it the basement. What I should have done was bury it in the back yard, never to see the light of day again.
As soon as the first round said "FIGHT" I might as well thrown in the towel. The game was gracious enough thou to just stand there for two seconds and let me get one shot in even thou I was 99.9% dead anyway, I mean, what the F is the point?!

I don't even think the skill level even works.
The only way to make it up the ladder without facing brutalities and stage fatalities is if you were to play as Kintaro, (he's your best bet.)
If you play as Motaro you will get your a** handed to you literally! (Pun intended). The A.I. actually puts any character to uppercut him and send him flying almost out of view of the screen! But if you were to uppercut the A.I. Motaro, he only staggers, can't have him flying into the air, oh no, that would require fairness in the game.

MK has been known for the copy and paste routine on characters, and MK Trilogy is the crowning achievement of it. The male and female ninjas, and if a guy has a bike helmet and armor pads that they bought down over at Wal-Mart and calling them cyborg ninjas, you know this is a cheap a** game. What the hell was this game made off of, a 50$ budget? I guess Wal-Mart was selling helmets and bike armor pads on a half off sale.

If the Matrix was to take a glitchy $***, I'm sure that it would look something like this. For every time it loads a match, I can actually hear my PS one groaning like it was trying to pass a cinder block.
If that PS could talk it'd say, "Oh lord... please... help me!" :(...

I can see now why it was in five dollar bin, but I remember that there was a trash bin next to it, and if I knew then before I bought it, I would have said; "Hey, this is in the wrong bin."

Its hard to take this gory game seriously if that douche bag Dan Fordan's big fat head keeps popping out saying "Toasty." I wouldn't be a damn bit surprised if I saw Charley Sheen pop-out of the corner and say; "Already got your money dude."

And when it says on the back of the game. "Seven humiliating stage fatalities that sends your opponent plummeting to there embarrassing death." That's you, your the opponent. The A.I. will make sure of that.

As for brutalities, who the hell takes the time to flush there life away to memorize twelve different moves just to do one lousy move so a guy explodes? Wow. I never seen five skulls and twenty femur bones pop out of a guy a before. Apparently Ed or any of the designers of this pile of $*#& game, doesn't know a damn thing about the human anatomy. Its bad enough that you have to remember all the fatalities and trademark moves, now this?

To sum it up.
The game play was awful.
The A.I. is cheap.
Glitchy as hell.
They copy and paste the characters as they always do.
The option menu is useless.
The video resolution looks like it was filmed through a potato.
The audio is awful.
And the story sucked.

Forget water-boarding. Forget Guantanamo bay. That would be considered a health spa compared to this game. S*#$, force a terrorist to play this game for 8 hours strait, and he'll tell you on how to cure any form of cancer.

I can't believe how these retailers on here are pricing this POS at $99.99. Hell, there's some retailers on here that are ranging this torture device, at $150.00. Yeah, good luck with that guys. You let me know how well that works out for ya.
Any poor bastard that was unfortunate enough that played this atrocious, inhumane, intolerable game, with a brain stem knows, this game couldn't be given away.

This game is ranked right next to the Jumanji board game, both should be thrown into the sacred ark, welded shut, put into a create, and shipped to a warehouse somewhere, and there it will be "dealt with TOP... MEN." Because this game will melt your face off while you're screaming in agony.

Looking back on my teenager days where I was young, dumb, full of ***, and didn't know any better, I was easily manipulated into crap like this. So of course I would defend games that I didn't know anything about. Of course I would deny that I've been taken advantage of. And now we see the same types of teens that are doing this all over again. They'll read this review and say, "Oh how dare you talk that way about MK! Even thou I didn't grow up with it... bla, bla, bla." I doubt that any one of them ever played this game on a PS 1. They'd argue that they'd played it of off their iphone or somethin', and say it's the same thing. NO! By then, the bugs have been fixed and it's NOT THE SAME. YOU'RE PLAYING IT FROM A PHONE! It has to be from a P...S...1! Hell to them they'd considered it a retro game. They can't judge a game accurately that wasn't in their childhood. All they can do is look at it from a future point of view. As for us 80's babies, we grew up with this crap. It's only right that we judge it from OUR timeline, no one else's. And it's hard even to this day, to let go of something that, even thou I've been scammed by marketing-ploys by some cheap broken product, I grew up with them. But ultimately, you have to say to yourself, that I've been had and it's time to let go.

I would not recommend this game to anyone, unless you enjoy yelling at your TV screen all night, then look no further.
But if you care about your sanity, and want to keep it intact. Then stay the hell away from this thing! You'll find no entertainment from it. And that's where it fails, it fails at what the main purpose of this game was made for, ENTERTAINMENT!

All it will do, is poison and scar your mind for life. You'll never be the same again.
You'll feel cheated, lied to, manipulated, used, frustrated, raped and robbed.
That's what you'll be left with. Enjoy.
top_profane.title: Mental Kombat !!!  LAMEALITY!
