top_rant.title: Not a clean install, but works

top_rant.text:
Rash, Zits and Pimple are your toads- your Battletoads!

The story- "One day Pimple and Princess Angelica were out cruisin' when suddenly Pimple and the Princess were captured by the Dark Queen and taken to a nearby planet. Meanwhile back on the spaceship vulture, the Battletoads base... "Hey toads! The Dark Queen's kidnapped Pimple and Angelica and she's holding them in the Gargantua. Go get 'em toads!"

"Yeah let's get them back! I gotta craving for action! Take us to the rumble, coach! "Not so fast Battlejerks! Before you even reach me you'll have to beat Robo-Manus and Big Blag. Still fancy your chances? Come to me now, if you dare! Hahahaha!" "I've located the Gargantua, toads! She's hidden beneath the planet's surface!"

"Now it's time to jam!"

Level 1- Ragnarok's Canyon. When your ship drops you off, you're in an open cave area with yellow spikes coming out of the ground, and dwarf creatures up to no good. Instantly you can tell the play control is really fast and tight. Battle a yellow pole coming out of the ground. The dwarfs come out of the back wall too. You can pick up sticks and smack them with it. You can also bash your shoulder into foes which shows how advanced the gameplay was by NES standards (along with the graphics- VERY nice). Watch out for gray dwarfs, flying pink pigs (you can ride on top of, cool!) and more thorn poles.

Suddenly a giant pink pole stomps its way in your direction and you find yourself in an underground red area. Music's really cool by the way. Pick up weapons and toss them at the machine gun fire. You return to the surface just to jump in a hole. "So Hookbill, you frogs did well! But let us see how they do against my birds of prey- pray the birds don't get them, hahahaha!" Hey who you callin' a frog? "Let's get mad, bad and crazy. Toads, use the turbo-cables to tackle that chasm!

Level 2- Wookie Hole. In this stage you descend from a swinging rope. Kick or sword swipe the crows, vultures and rising yellow snakes. Watch out for blue robots that blast out of the background rocks. You eventually find yourself near electrical currents. Swing past them. There's also green snakes and gray robots. Whoa boy- this stage is long and tough! "Give up now before the mutant ratpack gets you, wart-heads!" "Better step on it toads- the ratpack's hot on your heels!"

Level 3- Turbo Tunnel. Shoulder bash and punch the enemies in this underground cave area with really odd music. Punch out upright walking giant mouse creatures (basically humans with heads shaped like mice!) Jump aboard the jet ski and away you go! VERY fast level by NES standards. Avoid obstacles and fences. At least the obstacles flash giving you total warning before you approach them. Still, MAJOR hard level. The length alone will wear you down. A flying saucer comes by and drops obstacles at an alarmingly fast rate. Avoid bouncing blue fists. This stage gets ridiculously fast at the very end. "So, the rat rockets didn't get you huh? Well, the ice spikes certainly will!" "Brr, it's cold enough down there to freeze the yarbles off a Zalladian behometh!" I was thinking the same thing!

Level 4- Arctic Caverns. This stage begins with your toad character going down an icy hill avoiding falling spikes and sliding/spinning ice cubes. You can pick up the ice cubes. Groovy music too. Watch out for the snowball tossing snowmen. Sliding yellow porcupines and giant pink spike balls that resemble sea urchins are more obstacles in your way. There's also piles of tiny snowballs to pick up and throw, but this level is mainly about dodging snowman snowballs and jumping over sea urchins. The porcupines get intense near the end. "You know what they say don't you- out of the frying pan and into the surf! "Hang ten toads! Surf City here we come!"

Level 5- Surf City. Surf across the water avoiding bouncing logs. This level's pretty cool but not as cool as the previous racing stage. The spinning whirlpools make a flying saucer like sound which is odd. Punch out the yellow walking stick afterwards. A bouncing mouse with a chubby stomach is your boss fight! Beat him with your stick. Another water segment, this time avoiding spiked fish. "What?! You've made it through?! I'll make you pay for your impudence!" "Watch out toads! Sensors indicate something long and slippery slithering toward you!"

Level 6- Karnath's Lair. In this stage you ride and climb across a snake train as it takes you in all kinds of directions around the room. Jump through the out hole near the end, and enter another room with the same snake. This time there's spikes you have to swerve around. The pink background makes me think I'm in front of a giant lego, lol. Through the out hole, and into a dark blue room with the same train snake. Don't fall off, and don't run into the spikes! In the red lego room, it's the same idea once again. You're done "You're good. I'll give you that- good enough to burn! "Wow Toads! You'll be really hot and bothered by this little sizzler!"

Level 7- Volkmire's Inferno. Punch out our shoulder bash bouncing walking kangaroo enemies. Jump onto log platforms to move across. Watch out for flying saucers. Wow, the screen turns fiery and this appears REALLY cool on the NES. You're now flying a jet through vertical electrical beams. Avoid fireballs that come shooting several at a time. If there's a pattern to this it's a tricky one, and one I can't detect. Watch out for dropping and rising missiles. The pace picks up a LOT near the end, giving you little time to avoid the electrical beams. At least the beeping sound is a nice indication the beams are approaching. "The higher you climb swamp scum, the further you'll fall!" "When the goin' gets tough, the toads get goin'! Up and at 'em guys!"

Level 8- Intruder Excluder. In this case you have to climb back and forth moving across steel rafters while heading towards the top of a building in search of an opening. It gets tricky when you encounter electrical beams in between the spaces of the rafters, bouncing balls, trampoline bouncers, bouncing packs of dinner rolls, rafters moving at different speeds and floating electrical shooting brown foes. The boss fight is a gun shooting thing that looks like a monster and superhero combined. Shoulder bash him around the room. "Go through the steel spaghetti toads, and watch out for bbytraps!"

Level 9- Terra Tubes. You can ride propellers in this stage. Avoid electrical shooting foes and punch out gray creatures. Ride the propeller up and around the spikes. Watch out for the thin-layered rolling tires. They jump around and prove difficult to avoid. They actually follow your toad character around the room, even when you think you've avoided it. Goodness, this just makes Battletoads even more difficult! The tire chase makes up a huge chunk of the level too. You can now swim which is hilarious to see. Avoid the spikes and fish. The little golden piranhas... are bigger than they look! Oh golly! More tire-avoiding, but this time you're in the water so it's trickier (and slower, but not by much). These tires sure like to follow you around don't they! Oh there's even rubber duckies, haha! Avoid the sharks too. "Your arrogance would annoy me Battlebarf, if it wasn't for the fact that you're going to be bombing out very soon!" "Hey toads! You have 3 bombs to defuse before the ratpack blows us all to toad heaven!"

Level 10- Rat Race. Another building stage. This time you're descending down and punching out yellow misty steam foes. The frantic music is very annoying, as is the high pace of the level. Those floating electrical charge shooting enemies are back, and the walking kangaroos are even more annoying crammed in this tight space. Punch out the bomb when you arrive to that point, and head down the shifting rafters. More dangerous steam shooting at your face, and balls rolling around. The boss fight is with some spike vested bulky pig creature. Punch him around man! "Oh no! The bombs are all defused and the wartheads are still alive!" "There's a crazy roller-coaster comin' up toads, so keep a cool green head!" You mean a brown head? I'm not a frog remember!

Level 11- Clinger Winger. This is no ordinary roller-coaster! You're controlling a wheel while a spinning color-changing vortex thing follows you around the room. This is basically the same concept as the train snake from the previous level except this time you're on a narrow road instead of riding a snake. You actually have to fight the vortex as a boss. Pick him up, throw him around, punch, do whatever it takes but keep attacking it. "Top of the world toadies! Come and get me if you dare!" "Well it's one small step for a toad, one giant leap for Toadkin! Go to it guys!"

Level 12- the Revolution. You're on a spinning building in this level. Battle the rat looking foes and the spiked yellow balls while climbing bouncy platforms. What weird music this is. Not sure if it's dramatic or relaxing- the music sort of alternates between the two styles. Smack the big mouthed rats that blow dust balls at you. Punch out the flying green ghostly thing. What a WEIRD level- both the music and the enemies give me the creeps.

The platforms become numerous as the stage goes on, and the ball shooting brown creature floating on the side of the screen is annoying. He reminds me of Ridley from Metroid. This fashionable building is REALLY high man! It won't end! Bouncy platforms, bouncy ghosts and more. Now it's finally time for the final boss. The lady who's been talking smack to you the whole time (no pun intended, but not really a pun anyway since these are toads!) Yeah, time to fight her! The Dark Queen! She spins around as frantically as you can guess. She even vibrates around the room before turning into a tornado.

"Robo-Manus? Blaggie! Speak to your queen! Have those vile slush-hoppers crushed your craniums? Well don't worry- I'll restore you both. You snot gobblers will pay for this!" "Waydigo, toads! The big guy's back and the princess is saved!" You then see a cutscene of your toads climbing a rope and entering the ship to leave. "And so, the Dark Queen is defeated once again. Retreating into the shadowy margins of the galaxy to recoup her losses... until the next time..."

Wow, this game lives up to its reputation as being *insanely* difficult. Probably the hardest NES game ever, and that's really saying a lot considering there's at least 50 other insanely hard games on the console. The graphics are really appealing, the play control great, the music catchy and intense and I highly recommend this game. Just... don't expect to beat it! One of those games you play to see how far you get.
top_rant.title: battle on!

top_rant.text:
I'm really torn on how to feel about this game. It's good... but it's also deeply flawed.

[One thing I've learned about reviewing any game whatsoever: people get very insecure about anyone having a negative opinion about something they love, because they treat it as an attack on their own personal selves for some reason, going so far as to spit and cry and moan and whimper about it. It really shouldn't matter that much to you that my opinion is different than yours. With that said, don't bother trying to change my opinion. It will remain the same, no matter what you say, and my review will still exist]

Be aware: this review may contain spoilers.

Resident Evil VII is the revitalization of the original RE games, which were the first games I ever played on the original Playstation, and might as well symbolize the Playstation itself. They defined horror games for decades. They had an interesting blend of light problem-solving-- which I love-- with some creepy music and freaky enemies, all with a bit of trying your best to survive in an experiment gone wrong. These games were great for introducing people to survival horror, even if they were incredibly flawed in dialogue and character development.

Ever since Resident Evil 4 and it's widespread popularity, the games started taking a more "dudebro American action hero" approach, rather than survival and getting the crap scared out of you. They became less horror and more... well, "dudebro action" games. Gone was the terror you felt, and the haunting creep of chills up your spine when you hear a hunter creature hissing and walking down the next hallway. Now come and watch Leon spout one-liners about how cool he is while he kicks five Mexicans in the face. The Leon from Resident Evil 2 would look at Resident Evil 4 and 6 Leon like he was a maniac. Even Claire would, who still managed to keep a lot of her strengths in future titles she was in (Code: Veronica still remains to be my favorite RE title. Don't judge me. It was amazing-- flawed, but amazing).

I eventually stopped buying RE games. I rented 6 and found it laughably ridiculous, and didn't even get halfway through the game. I played a couple other titles with friends, like the rail-shooter Umbrella Chronicles. I owned 5 as it sort of came with the Xbox360, and cringed at it the whole time. NONE of them felt like RE anymore. No more creepy, ungodly experiments-- just ones that turn people into bigger and bigger swamp creatures.

Then we reach Resident Evil VII (now we're using Roman numerals because SHUT UP, IT FITS IN THE TITLE AND IT'S CLASSY!). And I have to say, I was nodding my head, thinking "This feels more like a horror game". But that feeling eventually seeped away... and I'll explain why.

CONS:
1. The entire game revolved around this family of stereotypical rednecks with monster superpowers. Does that sound scary or silly to you? Because to me, it was beyond the realm of ridiculous-- laughably so. I get that horror films use creepy "backwoods sheep-lovin' folk" as a lazy excuse to make them "creepy" a lot (Texas Chainsaw Massacre, The Hills Have Eyes, any B horror movie ever)... but this is not a film. It plays a lot like a film, with how often it interrupts your gameplay to have a chat with a character that gets really up in your face to play on the VR aspect of it in a painfully obvious way. But it's not the least bit scary to me. The funny thing is, these backwoods villains aren't even a huge part of what's supposed to be the hidden plot. They're a symptom of a much larger scheme that is revealed within the last hour of gameplay. So I felt that fighting them had no real purpose to the story, because it's revealed that they're just nobodies. And yes... I've played through the entire story.

2. The "main villain"-- or "big bad", if you will-- is not even a character you learn about and understand until the very end of the game, in I guess what is meant to be a "twist". The character who caused the very mess you got yourself into is not even properly discussed until the end. When I did find out who that character was, as well as her purpose, I groaned harder than I've ever groaned. To understand what I mean, think about these films: The Ring (1, 2, and 3), The Shining, The Omen, The Exorcist, Silent Hill (both the games and the movies), Orphan, Insidious, Sinister, Firestarter, Poltergeist, The Orphange, The Witch, The Good Son, Pet Sematary, Village of the Damned, Children of the Corn, The Grudge, The Wicker Man. Tell me... and think real hard now... what do aaaalllll of these horror films have in common? Once you figure it out, you'll immediately know the main antagonist of the game. And you'll groan, too.

3. Remember when Resident Evil games had a huge assortment of enemies, instead of just one type of static enemy that takes different forms?
Remember when we had zombie dogs, zombie people, giant plants, giant sharks, giant spiders, crows, hunters, tyrants...? The lists goes on and on. In Resident Evil VII, say hello to the "molded"! Again. And again. And again. And... oh, come on! The first time I saw one, I nearly pissed myself with terror. I actually screamed obscenities! Then I saw another one. And another one. And... well, as you might of guessed, I stopped being scared once I had my shotgun.
The enemy variety is one good thing I can say about the "dudebro action" versions of Resident Evil. At least they had more than just one type of thing to fight (and no, I'm not including bosses, obviously).

4. Ethan is slow as pudding when fighting, and in a tight space, it's a nightmare.

5. A BS "choice" you have to make near the end of the game that isn't a choice at all. Don't expect Deus Ex level decision-making, or BioWare style character development here. It just exists to change the ending cinematic, which is bland and rushed in either example. The ending doesn't change much anyway. The game also tried to make me feel "guilty" for my choice, too, even though at the time, I thought it was the perfect choice and the one that made the most sense. No, come to find out, the game set me up to make a black and white "good" or "bad" choice. I read somewhere that you make important decisions in this game that can change the ending. Well, that was kind of a lie, wasn't it?

6. As you probably guessed from the above statement, it has very low replay value. I've seen everything I need to see.

7. It was much shorter than I expected... though with the plot elements, I'm not that upset.

8. There was no laboratory. I saw a map at a later point in the game that got my hopes up and made me think I was on my way to deep lab where I got to see experiments gone awry. Nope. Nothing. No lab. No experiments (so to speak). Nothing cool like that. I really, really wanted one, and was let down tremendously. I realize that not all RE games have to have one... but everyone keeps saying that this is an homage to the classics. Well, if it's an homage to the classics, where's my lab (don't tell me the ship is the lab, because that's a crappy cop-out. It looked nothing and acted nothing like one)?

I'm sure plenty of people are butthurt over all of this, so let me lay out what I actually did like:

PROS:
1. It FEELS like Resident Evil, a lot more than the "dudebro action" games ever did. No more hulking Chris Redfiled punching a boulder of lava because MAN!! You're a vulnerable, weak and terrified guy set in an awful situation. You're trapped, and need to get out. It feels more horrifying than being able to dropkick enemies in the face. You feel like you're trying to survive. They got that part right, at least. Even the different types of keys that unlock certain doors was a staple of the old RE games that made them flavorful. I was glad to see it come back.

2. There's a VR option, which probably adds to depth. I didn't play it in VR, because I'll probably puke from motion sickness if I did.

3. The first person perspective, while obviously playing off of P.T.'s success, does work surprisingly well. For the first hour of gameplay, I was seriously creeped out, and one of those reasons was the perspective.

4. The playable flashbacks in the form of video tapes. Something about those were creepier and more unsettling than the main game, because you know that the character you're playing is going to die in some horrible way. You just have to reach that point. These added an extra bit of flavor to the game that I liked.

5. The graphics and atmosphere. The rooms and hallways all resembled the eerie set pieces of the original RE games. One that really reminded me of them was the hunting trophy room with the huge taxidermied deer in it. You went in that room and were able to imagine what it was used for in the past.

Overall, I did like the game. I really did. But I didn't like it enough to want any kind of sequel, or even to play it a second time, unless it's been years and I've forgotten all about it-- which is likely what will happen. There was just too much about it that put a foul taste in my mouth that sort of let down all of my hopes. I did get scared in the beginning, but then it wore off immediately, and my fear was replaced with annoyance. It did vastly improve upon all of the things I hated about the more recent RE titles. It's a good game, but not a great one by any means. It does play homage to the classics, which I greatly appreciate, but it also plays too hard on cliches and tropes that I'm really sick of by now.

It's probably best suited for people that want an intro to horror who have never seen a horror game or movie in their life.
top_rant.title: Good... But Really Not Great [Long, Detailed Review]

top_rant.text:
GRAN TURISMO 5: MY REVIEW

As an intermediate driver in GT games, I find myself in the middle of the skill chain. It is so much fun to play a driving game that challenges you to control your car before your tear off at high speeds. GT is a driving simulator not a driving game. It is so beautifully done in terms of car physics and braking/accelleratin physics. I love to drive and with alot of practice maybe I'll get better. So when this game launched, I was generally excited to dive in and show off my mediocracy. Gran Turismo 5 has been the focus of many PS3 gamers for years now. The long production time on this game should prove it's worth in gold upon playing right? Well, for the most part that is completely true. But there are some flaws in the game design and content that are the only things holding this title back from one of the greatest games ever. I will start off by giving a guidline to the game and then discuss pros/cons and features.

Scope:

The overall scale of the game captures a vast mass of options and experiences. From simple drives to grinding endurances and from jaw dropping to "What the heck?", Gran Turismo 5 covers a wide range of playable features. This gives the gamers a game that will not end with 60 hours of play. More like 6 months!

Features-

A-Spec:
The main mode in Gran Turismo 5 is GT Mode. And the main career mode is A-Spec. This is where you spend a majority of the time. It features severall tiers of events ranging from Beginner to Endurance. Each event has specific regulations and car types to enlist in the race. A handful of the races actually don't have limitations, thus allowing for some power maxed cars in your garage to feed off of the weaker computer cars. We've all done it, feel no guilt. :) You gain EXP for the races and acrue Credits to purchase cars and upgrades. Also, winning the events unlocks cars for your garage. A-Spec level/rank goes up to 40.

Pros:
-Great variety of races and race types
-Good way to learn the tracks and play a wide variety of them
-Some races are generous in credits for little effort
-Fun to drive many different cars and in different races to find the tunings that work best
-Some Endurance races are a great deal of fun in just a few hours. A great chance to learn the track inside and out.

Cons:
-The levels/ranks. I am a level 31 and the task is gruelling to level up to 32, let alone from 39 to 40. I was fine with the EXP amounts until level 30. It felt like it was balanced and took a perfect amount of time. But now I look aT the million EXP needed to rank up and see that the only way to reach the next level, and the next, and then make it to 40 is to tediously grind out the same races for months and months. I agree it shouldn't take 5 days to reach level 40 but it shouldn't have to rely completely on grinding endurance races forever either. I don't have the time to sit and run 3,4,or 9 hour races all the time.
-Balance of credit rewards. Some races have the stupedist amount of credits. Example: The American Championship event in the Expert tier on Indy nets you 117,000 credits in roughly 4 minutes. Yet grinding the first endurance race for 2 hours nets you only 184,000..........
-Endurance races. I love the first four races. But this con focuses on the 24 hour events. 24 hours! I will never be able to do that in the first place, given nor will I opt to leave my Playstation running for that long. The 24 Nurburgring event also unlocks at level 40. Why? Why not level 39 to help you GET to level 40?
-The NASCAR events are subpar. To a hardcore nascar fan or an excellent GT driver this race is okay, but for an intermediate driver, the Daytona race will piss you off to no end. Why is it so hard to hold a draft through the corners? I know I could do it millions of time until its a piece of cake, but I don't have the urge to. I beat all of the other races in that event on the first try, and by a long shot.

B-Spec:
Offers the chance to manage an AI driver through the same races as A-Spec. Level/rank goes up to 40 as well.

Pros:
-Unlocking some good cars
-Its humorous to watch your driver when he's mad.(before frustration overtakes you)
-The concept seems like a good idea.

Cons:
-Races are twice as long
-Your drivers are complete rubbish. If they get hot headed at all they drive like an over emotional teenager. They over rev the gears and floor it coming out of corners. And sometimes you can give them a car that over maxed the others on the course by 150hp and they don't overtake when you tell them to.
-Who really wants to surrender the wheel to the computer and watch?......

License Tests:
Tests that teach you the skills necessary to drive well throughout the game.

Pros:
-Most are very valuable lessons to learn
-Great variety of cars to use teaches you to handle different setups and speeds
-LOTS OF THEM!
-Gold, Silver, and Bronze trophy system

Cons:
-You only get a few hundred EXP for most
-No credits
-Most are very hard to Gold. Some seem impossible to any driver other than GT5 pros.

Special Events:
Events that showcase certain vehicles and driving experiences. Kart Racing, NASCAR, Rally, Mercedes AMG cars, and the X1 race car are among them. Others include a few races on the Top Gear Test Track, Sebastian Loeb rally and a Grand Tour of Italy.

Pros:
-Fun, Fun, Fun!
-Good amount of EXP
-Driving the X1 is insane!
-Kart racing is hilarious
-Grand Tour event is fun
-The Nurburgring event in the rain with no driving line will elevate your heart rate the same as watching a dozen scary movies. Good times. :)

Cons:
-Some races are so hard!
-Rally races are tricky on the gravel and hard to gold trophy, yet I won by over a minute in the snow with the same car.....
-Sebastian Loeb challenge is hard.....enough said
-The X1 race is fun, but insanely difficult
-The infamous Lotus challenge on the Top Gear Test Track. There is no way that any car handles that poorly in real life.

THE CARS:

Visual:
Yes, there are over 1,000 cars in Gran Turismo 5. At first, I thought this was amazing. Now, I think it's a waste. The dealerships are seperated into 3 types. Main Dealership is the premium cars, used dealership is the standard cars, and online dealership is popular or hard to find cars. The cars in the premium dealership are so amazing. Some are rendered with such beauty that I find myself just driving around to look at the cockpit view. The level of detail in the premium cars exceeds all expectations I had. Polyphany Digital did it right. It really does feel like you are sitting in a real car! The standard cars are still fun to drive, but are not as fun to look at of course.

Audio:
The cars sound pretty great overall, but some sound fake or whiny in the cockpit view. Don't know why...

Volume of Cars:
This is what gets me. There are 200 premium cars and over 800 standard. Most of the standard are just different years of eachother or slightly different model. In the used car dealership, there is a over flooding of similar cars. So many Imprezas, Lancers, GTOs, Skylines, Fairladys. How many do we really need? They should have cut 300 cars from the list of standard and made 100 more premiums. There are some great vehicles to buy standard though. But for the most part, it's the same type of cars always in the used dealership and never seems to be the one you need or want. In the premium dealorship, there are some amazing cars. For me, the inclusion of Ferrari's was huge. These are my favorite cars to drive. The variety is great too. Whether you like the small cars with low HP or powerful cars that eat tracks alive, there is something for you. The only downside is there are some really expensive ones! :) While I'm talking expensive, here's another thought. Some standard cars are 500,000 credits or more! Some are over 10,000,000!!!! Yet, in the premium dealership, there are cars for 20,000.......... ANY CAR OVER 500,000 SHOULD BE PREMIUM!!!

Pros:
-Variety is awesome
-Ferrari's and other rare cars
-Great graphics
-Endless array of cars to choose from leaves you giddy with new buyer confusion :)

Cons:
-Too many same style standard cars
-Too many stupid Skylines....haha
-Expensive standard cars should be premium if you have to pay an arm and a leg for them
-Lots of crappy standard cars that aren't worth buying

Tuning/Customizing:
There are some great ways to tune your cars and alter your cars. You can change oil, paint, add aero kits, rebuild engines, race mod, and even wash your car. (Haven't found a reason yet) In the tuning shop, you can purchase tires and upgrades to your engine, transmission, suspension, exhaust, and other components. The customizable transmission is a must for me on premium cars. You can buy the necessary upgrades to tune your car the way YOU want it! Except for brakes, for some reason.

Pros:
-Fun to alter settings until the car drives perfect
-Good variety of tires to use on different tracks. Sports and comfort tires to help learn to drive the car better are nice before tossing on the old racing shoes.
-Painting the kart is humorous. Just watch them spray the entire driver. haha

Cons:
-Can't upgrade brakes
-Can be spendy to upgrade in the early ranks
-Turbo stages on some cars don't add that much HP for a lot of credits

Damage:
The Gran Turismo series is notorious for having no damage. Come on Polyphany Digital! You took 6 years to make this game and still crummy damage physics? From what I understand, it unlocks later in the game........why? You hit a car going 180 and it sounds like cardboard! Not the end of the world, though.

Track Editor:
Fun feature allowing you to make custom tracks. You don't have a whole lot of control but enough to make some great tracks. Booklet said you can race them online but I haven't figured out how. You can change the number of sections, difficulty per section, width of road, and sharpness of turns.

Pros:
-Good amount of freedom to make fun tracks
-Several different themes including dirt and snow and even kart tracks
-You can make some hard tracks with epic difficulty to the sections
-Share tracks with your friends!

Cons:
-I wish you could have more freedom

Photo Travel Mode:
This allows you to go to special locations to take pictures of your premium cars. It is actually more fun than it sounds. YOu can setup shots and move the car and even zoom and adjust the frame rates and other camera features. You can also share the pics with friends and export them to your PS3 album to use as wallpaper. There is also a feature that allows you to go into photo mode while watching a replay. It's awesome.

Online:
The online multiplayer in this game is epic. I love to just drive and race my friends in lobbies or hook up for races against other strangers. Most of the time I have fun just setting lap times and having all my friends compete for best lap. You don't get many credits but it doesn't matter. It's so much fun to race! You can favorite 100 cars from your garage and use them online or choose from the recommended list. Regulations and restrictions can be set to things like HP or even car specific. This is my favorite part of the game. I am still planning to get my friends together for an endurance race on the Nurburgring in Karts!! haha The racing is lag free and the overall fun factor is through the roof!

Pros:
-Great experience
-Lag free
-lots of custom regulations
-tons of tracks
-Penalties for rude, aggressive, and cheap drivers
-100 favorited cars + recommended list
-overall well behaved community. With the exception of the occasional little kid that rolls a Zonda into your Fiat 500 race. But you can kick them. :)
-Spectating the race is humorous!

Cons:
-Not much credit/EXP. Oh well...
-ummmmmm.....

CONCLUSION:

Overall, Gran Turismo 5 lives up to the name. It has consumed me and left me wanting to play more, even though it takes forever to level up. Though there are minor flaws in the game, they are still flaws. The only real dissapointing thing about it is the addition of more features instead of expansion of the basic ones. B-spec could just be eliminated in favor of more A-spec races and events. They should have cut the car list down and turned several hundred standard cars into premium cars. The Gold trophies in special events and license test could be a bit more forgiving to the average drivers. And the damage and sound could stand for improvement. But even with those flaws in game design, Gran Turismo 5 gives gamers a brilliant array of driving experiences to endure and captures the art of driving well. I recommend buying this game if you have any interest in driving games. It's a great buy. If you enjoy the arcade like driving games like Need for Speed or others, rent it because it is nothing like them. It's a real driving simulator! I can't stop playing this game. In fact, it's time to stop this review because I just looked at the case and I wanna play......
top_rant.title: Let's Talk Gran Turismo 5

top_screaming.text:
2-13-2020. A FEW DAYS AGO I BOUGHT ADOBE PHOTOSHOP ELEMENTS 2020 TO DO SOME HIGH LEVEL EDITING OF IMAGES. TODAY THE SOFTWARE DISK ARRIVED AND I INSTALLED IT. INSTALLATION WENT SMOOTH. THEN THERE WAS AN "UPDATE SOFTWARE TO THE LATEST" MESSAGE AND I CLICKED ON IT. THE PROGRAM STARTED TO RUN THE UPDATE INSTALLATION BUT THEN CAME BACK WITH A WINDOW THAT SAID "Update conflict" Please save your work and close the following programs for all logged in users, then click "Continue". Adobe Photoshop Elements 2020 (Error Code: 131) Then there was a "Get Help" Link. AND AT THE BOTTOM OF THE WINDOW WAS A "Quit" and "Continue" OPTION. IF YOU CLICKED THE "Continue" LINK THE PROGRAM WENT BACK TO THE INSTALL PROCESS AGAIN. AND A FEW MINUTES LATER THE SAME "Update conflict" WINDOW APPEARED. THIS HAPPENED REPEATEDLY. SO, I DECIDED TO CLICK ON THE "Get Help" LINK. THAT TOOK ME TO A WIKI PAGE THAT DESCRIBED THE ELEMENTS 2020 AS A REGISTRY PROBLEM. THEN IT HAD A PICTURE OF A SOFTWARE BOX LABELED WITH "REGISTRY REPAIR." SO, IT LED ONE TO BELIEVE THAT ALL THEIR PROBLEMS WOULD BE FIXED BY BUYING THE "REGISTRY REPAIR" SOFTWARE. LET ME TELL YOU AS ONE WHO HAS WORKED WITH COMPUTERS SINCE 1979, AND MY FIRST OPERATING SYSTEM WAS DOS 1.1, AND A OS CALLED CMP WHICH BECAME DOS 1.1 THROUGH BILL GATES GENIUS. IN WINDOWS OS, ESPECIALLY FROM WINDOW 95 ON, THE LAST THING YOU WANT TO DO IS MESS WITH THE REGISTRY UNLESS YOU REALLY DO KNOW WHAT YOU ARE DOING. I'VE HAD THESE "REGISTRY REPAIR" AND "REGISTRY MECHANIC" TOTALLY CORRUPT MY OPERATING SYSTEMS WHERE THEY BECAME USUSABLE. FORTUNATEL, I ALWAY HAD FULL BACKUP IMAGES OF C: DRIVE. THERE ARE SOME EXCELLENT, I MEAN REALLY GOOD COMPLETE C: DRIVE BACKUP WITH IMAGE PROGRAMS OUT THERE. MY TWO FAVORITE ARE MACRIUM REFLECT AND ACRONIS.

SO, I WASN'T ABOUT TO GO THE ROUTE OF "FIXING THE REGISTRY." BUT NO-WHERE ON THE PHOTOSHOP ELEMENTS 2020 PAGE COULD I FIND A "SUPPORT" LINK THAT WOULD GIVE ME A PHONE NUMBER OF LINK TO REAL TECHNICAL SUPPORT. I DECIDED THEN THAT I WOULD TRY DOING A BING SEARCH FOR ADOBE TECHNICAL SUPPORT AND IF THAT WAS NOT SUCCESSFUL I WOULD RETURN THE PRODUCT TO AMAZON. I BUY MOST ALL BUT MY GROCERIES FROM AMAZON BECAUSE OF THEIR EXCELLENT CUSTOMER FIRST POLICIES.

SEARCHING WITH BING, I DID FIND A LINK AND I WENT TO IT AND IT BROUGHT ME TO CUSTOMER SUPPORT. WITH JUST A LITTLE CLICKING AROUND I CAME TO ACTUAL TECHNICAL SUPPORT AND A CHAT LINE WITH A REAL HUMAN BEING. I COULDN'T DETECT HIS/HER ACCENT BECAUSE EVERYTHING WAS IN TYPE. I HAD EXPERIENCED DELL TECH. SUPPORT IN YEARS PAST. AND I SPOKE WITH A HUMAN BEING. BUT I COULD NEVER GET THROUGH THE BENGALI ACCENT. SO I GAVE UP ON DELL AS DELL GAVE UP ON ME THE CUSTOMER WHEN I NEEDED TECH SUPPORT.

TO KEEP IT SHORTER, ADOBE TOOK CONTROL OF MY COMPUTER AND FIXED THE PROBLEM WITHIN 30 MINUTES. I WASN'T WORRIED THAT HE MIGHT MAKE A MISTAKE BECAUSE I MAKE REGULAR BACKUP IMAGES WITH MACRIUM REFLECT (OR YOU COULD USE ACRONIS), SO ANY DISASTER COULD BE EASLY RECOVERED FORM. TAKE IT FROM SOMEONE WITH OVER FOUR DECADES EXPERIENCE WITH PCs, GET YOURSELF A GOOD BACKUP IMAGE CREATING PROGRAM AND A GOOD WESTERN DRIVE OR SEAGATE DRIVE EXTERNAL, WITH 2-4 TB, AND MAKE REGULAR BACKUPS, SO THAT IF THE WORSE HAPPENS, IT WON'T BE THE WORSE BECAUSE WITHIN 45 MINUTES YOU CAN BE BACK IN OPERATION.

I DON'T KNOW HOW YOU CAN FIND OUT WHAT COMPUTER EQUIPMENT/SOFTWARE COMPANIES HAVE REAL HONEST HUMAN INTERFACE TECHNICAL SUPPORT. MOST NO LONGER DO. I KNOW I WAS PAYING HP AROUND $50. PER YEAR FOR REAL HUMAN TECH. SUPPORT. BUT NOW, ITS ALL DONE WITH SOFTWARE QUERRIES THAT PUT YOU IN AN ENDLESS DO-LOOP. SO I MIGHT AS WELL BUILD AGAIN MY NEXT COMPUTER SINCE IT CAN BE DONE CHEAPER AND BETTER THAN BUYING COMMERCIAL ONES WHERE YOU GET NO REAL HUMAN CONTACT TECHNICAL SUPPORT. THIS IS ALL MY PERSONAL OPINION. I COULD BE WRONG. I AM ONLY SPEAKING FROM PERSONAL EXPERIENCE BUT IT IS STILL MY PERSONAL OPINION. MY PERSONAL OPINION IS THAT I WILL SOON BE BUYING ADOBE'S VIDEO EDITOR SOFTWARE BECAUSE, BASED ON TODAY'S EXPERIENCE, I FEEL I CAN TRUST THEM TO GIVE ME REAL HUMAN-CONTACT TECHNICAL SUPPORT.

IN ENDING THIS REFLECTION ON MY PERSONAL OPINION, I THINK IF YOU WILL PUT INTO AMAZON'S SEARCH ENGINE, BACKUP SOFTWARE, AND EXTERNAL HARD DISK, YOU WILL COME UPON ALL YOU WILL NEED AT A REASONABLE PRICE, TO BACK UP YOUR OPERATING SYSTEM, AND FILES IF YOU WANT TO, JUST IN CASE THE WORSE UNEXPECTADLY HAPPENS.
top_screaming.title: ADOBE GIVES SUPERIOR TECH SUPPORT FOR IT'S SOFTWARE, I KNOW FROM DIRECT EXPERIENCE

top_screaming.text:
I HAVE PLAYED FINAL FANTASY VII EVERYDAY SINCE IT CAME OUT THOSE MANY YEARS AGO. I'VE BEATEN IT COUNTLESS DOZENS OF TIMES AND THE ORGASMIC PLEASURE DERIVED FROM EVERY SECOND OF THIS DIVINE PIECE OF ART HAS YET TO DECREASE, IN FACT IT GETS GREATER. EVERY TIME I SELECT A NEW ATTACK, EVERY TIME I CLICK TO CONTINUE THE SPEECH TREE EVERY SECOND I WANDER ACROSS THE BEATIFUL AND BRILLIANT LANDSCAPE WAITING FOR YET ANOTHER TITILLATING RANDOM ENCOUNTER IS AN EXPERIENCE THAT I WILL TREASURE FOR THE REST OF MY LIFE. YOU HAVE LIVED AND DIED IN VAIN IF YOU GO THROUGH YOUR LIFE WITH OUT EXPERIENCING THIS UNPARALELLED JOY.

BUT THERE IS SO MUCH MORE THAN JUST JOY. THE FIRST TIME THAT AERIS DIED I I WAS SO STUNNED THAT I HAD A STROKE AND A HEART ATTACK SIMULTANEOUSLY WHILE VOMITING AND POOPING MYSELF, AS THE TEARS STREAMED DOWN MY FACE. IT WAS SO INTENSE THAT I PASSED OUT, AND SLIPPED INTO A COMA. FORTUNATELY, MY DEAR FRIENDS SET UP A PLAYSTATION IN MY ROOM AND PLACED THE CONTROLLER IN MY FLACID, COMATOSE HANDS ALLOWING ME TO CONTINUE THE GODLY ADVENTURE. THE SERENE YET POWERFUL, BEAUTIFUL YET FRIGHTENING MUSIC TURNED MY COMA DREAMS INTO JOYOUS VISIONS OF WONDER, WHICH MADE ME WISH NEVER TO AWAKE, WERE IT NOT FOR MY URGENT NEED TO COMPLETE THE MYSTIC QUEST.

THE COMBAT IS A SPECTACULARLY INTENSE, CEREBRAL AFFAIR. THE EARTH-SHATTERING VISUAL EFFECTS AND SPELLS LEAVE ME WEAK AND NAUSEATED EVERY TIME, YET I CANNOT, AND WILL NOT STOP, NO MATTER THE PRICE. WORDS COULD NOT ATTEMPT TO DO JUSTICE TO THE INTENSITY OF EVEN THE CALMEST FINAL FANTASY BATTLE.

THE STORY IS LIKE NO OTHER, THE SPECTACULAR DIALOGUE IS AN EMULSIFICATION OF THE GREATEST ASPECTS OF SHAKESPEARE, CHAUCER, ELIOT, TENNYSON, DONNE, HOMER, MARLOW, PLATO, ARISTOTLE, HARDY, FAULKNER AND JOYCE ROLLED INTO A PACKAGE SO MALIFLUENT THAT EACH OF THESE GREAT WRITERS WOULD DESTROY THEIR WORK FROM SHAME. THE PLOT CREATES A RICH TAPESTRY OF JOY, WONDER, FEAR AND AWE THAT LEAVES MY HEAD SPINNING, MY HEART POUNDING, MY BOWELS CLENCHING, MY HAIR RAISED, MY STOMACH TURNING AND ROILING, MY KNEES SHAKING AND MY EXTREMITIES COLD AND SHIVERING, WHILE A JOYOUS PASSION STILL BURNS FROM WITHIN. THE CHARACTERS ARE UNIVERSAL ARCHETYPES OF EVERY MAN, WOMAN AND CHILD YET THEY ARE UTTERLY DISTINCT, UTTERLY ALIVE, CHILLINGLY REAL, LOVABLE AND EROTIC TO THE END. THE STOIC, HEROIC AND CHRIST-LIKE CLOUD STANDS AS A SYMBOL OF ALL THAT IS GOOD AND TRUE IN HUMANITY, EARTH AND ALL OF ETERNITY. THE LOVABLE, GRUFF 'N TUMBLE BARRET IS CLASSIC EXAMPLE OF THE INDOMITABLE NEGRO SPIRIT. AERIS IS A FRAIL FLOWER OF BEAUTY AND WONDER, A REPRESENTATION OF THE LOSS AND SACRIFICE THAT INEVITABLY OCCURS BEFORE ONE REACHES THE APEX OF TRUE ENLIGHTENMENT. SEPHIROTH IS AS BLACK AS THE DEEPEST PITS OF HELL, AN EMBODIMENT OF THE SATANIC, UNGODLY, BLASPHEMOUS AND PROFANE FORCES WHICH SEEK TO CRUSH THE FRAIL YET UNBENDING ROSE OF THE RIGHT. STILL, ONE AS MEEK AS I COULDN'T HOPE TO ANALYZE ALL THE INTRICACIES, METAPHORS, CHIASMAS', SIMILES, HYPERBOLES, ANALOGIES, FABLES AND PARABLES WHICH FILL UP THIS TEXT, WHICH IS EASILY THE DENSEST AND RICHEST OF ALL KNOWN HUMAN AND ALIEN LANGUAGES.

ALL THE BEAUTY, TRUTH, MEANING, WONDER, SIGNIFICANCE AND INTEREST IN ALL THE WORLD ARE AS NOTHING ONCE YOU HAVE DISCOVERED THIS HIDDEN TREASURE. I FOR ONE WOULD TRADE ALL MY REMAINING DAYS FOR BUT ONE MORE MINUTE OF FINAL FANTASY VII INSPIRED PLEASURE, RATHER THAN BEING FORCED TO LIVE A HOLLOW, BITTER, JOYLESS EXISTENCE WITHOUT IT.

top_screaming.text: THIS WAS THE SINGLE WORST PURCHASE I HAVE MADE IN THE LAST 20 YEARS.  I HAVE BEEN A GAMER FOR OVER 25 YEARS AND THIS MACHINE HAS TURNED ME OFF GAMING SO BAD I DOUBT I EVER WASTE TIME AND MONEY ON VIDEO  GAMES AGAIN.  EVERY SINGLE GAME REQUIRES U TO BE LOGGED ON TO YOUR XBOX ACCOUNT WHILE YOU PLAY. WHICH MEANS YOU HAVE TO HAVE A MICROSOFT EMAIL ACCOUNT.  I GUESS THEY WANT TO MINE DATA FROM YOU AND MONITOR EVERYTHING U DO WHEN YOU PLAY A VIDEO GAME. WHEN DID PLAYING A STUPID VIDEO GAME BECOME SO DIFFICULT??  I CAN ONLY  MANAGE TO LOG ON ABOUT 30% OF THE TIME. XBOX LIVE IS CONSTANTLY CRASHING. IF ITS NOT DOWN THEN ABOUT HALF THE TIME THE GAME I AM PLAYING NEEDS ME TO BE LOGGED INTO THEIR SERVERS AND THEY ARE DOWN.  SO BASICALLY I HAVE TO GO THRU MY INTERNET PROVIDER, THEN AN XBOX SERVER, THEN ONTO MY GMAES SERVER JUST IN ORDER TO PLAY A GAME....GEE I WONDER WHY ITS SO SLOW....??I HAVE AVERAGE INTERNET SPEED AND CAN WATCH TV AND MOVIES, AND SURF ONLINE WITH EASE.  I WILL SHUT EVERYTHING DOWN AND TURN THE XBOX ON.  IT STILL WONT LOG ON TO XBOX LIVE ABOBUT 70% OF THE TIME.  RIGHT OUT OF THE BOX IT NEEDED A 10 GIG UPDATE THAT TOOK ABOUT 3 WEEKS TO COMPLETE.  SO FAR  EVERY GAME I HAVE PLAYED NEEDED AN UPDATE TO PLAY.  ONE TOOK 2 MONTHS TO UPDATE...I COULD NOT BELIEVE I BOUGHT A GAME OF THE YEAR EDITION ONLY TO FIND OUT ALL THE CONTENT NEEDED  TO STILL BE DOWNLOADED  FROM ONLINE AND NOT ACTUALLY ON THE DISC ITSELF...WHAT A JOKE!!  WHAT'S EVEN WORSE THAN THAT WAS THE DVD/BLU RAY PLAYER. I GO TO PLAY A DVD AND IT TELLS ME THAT THE SOFTWARE FOR THE DVD DRIVE NEEDS TO BE...GUESS WHAT....DOWNLOADED!!!  YOU PAY 350 SOME ODD DOLLARS ON A MACHINE AND YOU HAVE TO DOWN LOAD THE SOFTWARE TO MAKE IT RUN...STUPID!! IT TOOK 4 WEEKS FOR THAT SOFTWARE TO DOWNLOAD!  SAD THAT A 16 YEAR OLD PS2 HAS THAT SOFTWARE ALREADY ON IT RIGHT OUT OF THE BOX YET A NEW 350$ XBOX CANT SEEM TO MANAGE THE SAME FEAT. THE SIMPLE FACT THAT XBOX WANTS EVERYONE TO BE CONNECTED AND SOCIAL IS ONE OF THE BIGGEST DOWNFALLS OF THE INDUSTRY.  THEY DONT HAVE ENOUGH INTERNET POWER TO SERVE EVERYONE PROPERLY WHICH MAKES THE GAMING EXPERIENCE AWFUL.  NOT EVERYONE HAS THE SAME INTERNET SPEED AND IF YOURS IS LIKE MINE ONLY AVERAGE THEN IT MAKES THE XBOX ONE PRETTY MUCH USLESS.  THE GOOD OLD DAYS OF BUYING A GAME, COMING HOME AND STICKING IT IN THE MACHINE AND PLAYING ARE OVER AND THAT IS SAD.  WHILE WRITING THIS REVIEW I HAVE TRIED CONNECTING TO XBOX LIVE 8 TIMES AND NO LUCK.  I HAVENT BEEN ABLE TO PLAY ANY OF MY GAMES FOR THREE DAYS STRAIGHT NOW.  I HAVE A FEELING THIS MACHINE WILL END UP BEING  USED AS TARGET PRACTICE IN THE BACKYARD SOON.  I AM SURE I WILL HAVE NO PROBLEM CONNECTING IT TO SOME BULLETS!!!! GOD I AM GETTING SICK OF TECHNOLOGY!
top_screaming.title: PIECE OF JUNK! DONT BUY UNLESS U HAVE PREMIUM INTERNET SERVICES & TONS OF TIME TO WASTE WAITING TO LOG ON

top_screaming.text: I LOVE THE CONSOLE OR SHOULD I SAY THE GAMES THAT ARE REALEASED FOR THIS CONSOLE, HOWEVER, THE HARDWARE AND CUSTOMER SUPPORT ARE TOTALLY HORRIBLE... ONCE UPON A TIME I WAS AN EXCLUSIVE SONY FANBOY, EVER SINCE A LITTLE GAME CALLED FINAL FANTASY 7 WAS RELEASED. THEN FINALLY JUMPED OVER TO X-BOX WHEN THE MASTER CHIEF HAD HIS SECOND ADVENTURE. WHEN THE 360 WAS RELEASED I PURCHASED IT AND SOLD IT WITHIN A WEEK BECAUSE AT THE TIME A $400 HUNDRED DOLLAR UPGRADE THAT ONLY MADE GAMES LOOK MORE SHINY (G.R.A.W AND FULL AUTO) WAS TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE. THAT WAS UNTIL I PLAYED GEARS OF WAR LATE LAST SUMMER OF '07. I IMMEDIATLY PURCHASED ANOTHER CONSOLE AND IN FACT ALSO BOUGHT A 52" DLP T.V JUST TO PLAY IT LIKE IT WAS MEANT TO BE PLAYED. AND LIFE WAS GOOD... UNTIL 4 MONTHS DOWN THE ROAD, I JUST GOT HALO 3 AND BAM! RED RING OF DEATH... I CROSSED MY FINGERS AND HOPED FOR THE BEST AS I CALLED MICROSOFT, OF COURSE THESE DAYS ALL CALL CENTERS ARE OUT SOURCED IN INDIA OR SOMEWHERE. THEY ACTUALLY MANAGED TO HELP ME OUT, THE CUSTOMER SERVICE REP SAID THAT MY WARRANTY WILL BE EXTENDED FOR ANOTHER 3 YEARS AND THAT THEY WERE GOING TO SEND ME A BOX TO SHIP MY "BOX" IN. SENT MY "BOX" OFF, RECIEVED ANOTHER ONE NO FUSS AND LIFE WAS GOOD. LIFE HAS BEEN REALLY REALLY GOOD WITH GAMES LIKE MASS EFFECT, ASSAIN'S CREED, COD 4, BIOSHCOK, AND RECENTLY GEARS OF WAR 2 AND FALLOUT 3, JUST TO NAME A FEW!  UNTIL YESTERDAY, I JUST FINISHED DOWNLOADING THE NEW XBOX INTERFACE THING WHEN SUDDENLY BAM!? RED RING OF DEATH, AGAIN!? IT WAS LIKE BEING IN A VERY GOOD RELATIONSHIP AND FOUND OUT YOU WERE GETTING CHEATED ON NOT ONCE BUT TWICE! I WANT TO SMASH THE THING AT THIS POINT. SO I TRY NOT TO PANIC, CALLED INDIA AGAIN AND THIS TIME THE REP WAS VERY UNHELPFUL BECAUSE OF THE LANGUAGE BARRIER AND THE VAGUE FEELING OF UNEASE THAT THIS TIME THINGS WILL NOT TURN OUT LIKE THE LAST. THE REP TELLS ME THAT MY 'BOX IS REGISTERED UNDER SOMEONE ELSE'S NAME AND THAT MY WARRANTY HAS EXPIRED... BUT HE WAS STILL GOING TO PUT IN THE SERVICE REQUEST. HE GIVES ME THE SERVICE REQUEST NUMBER AND TELLS ME TO CALL BACK TOMORROW BECAUSE OF SOME TECHNICAL PROBLEMS THEY WERE HAVING IN THEIR SYSTEM (HOW IRONIC NOW THAT I THINK ABOUT IT). I WAS SO FRUSTRATED BY THE TIME I GOT OFF THE PHONE I WAS REALLY GOING TO SMASH MY 360 AND JUST BUY A PLAYSTATION AND BE DONE WITH IT... SO ANYONE CONSIDERING PURCHASING AN X-BOX 360 "USED" BE AWARE THAT THERE IS A VERY HIGH PROBABILITY OF THE RED RING OCCURENCE, AND THAT YOU MAY NOT BE ABLE TO GET IT REPAIRED UNDER WARRANTY, THIS IS MY SECOND R.O.D AND BROTHER HAS HAD A COUPLE AS WELL.
top_screaming.title: DO NOT BUY USED! RED RING OF DEATH!

top_screaming.text:
I PLAYED THE ORIGINAL PS2 "SNIPER ELITE" AND REALLY ENJOYED IT! WHEN AMAZON.COM ANNOUNCED THEIR RELEASE OF PS3 "SNIPER ELITE V2", I QUICKLY PRE-ORDERED AND WAITED FOR IT TO ARRIVE IN THE MAIL. ONCE THE GAME DISC WAS INSERTED INTO MY PS3 CONSOLE, IT NEVER CAME OUT UNTIL I HAD WON THE "SNIPER ELITE TROPHY" AT TOP LEVEL. THE OVERALL GRAPHICS AND THE WWII UNIFORMS, MOST EXCELLENT! THE MISSION(S) ALONG WITH THE STORY LINE I REALLY ENJOYED. NOW THAT I HAVE COMPLETED THIS ENTIRE GAME, AND KNOWING IT'S SECRETS AND DEALING WITH IT'S ONLY PROBLEM I WILL ENJOY IT EVEN MORE IN THE FUTURE!
THE ONLY PROBLEM I ENCOUNTERED WITH "SNIPER ELITE V2", THE HAND CONTOLLER RESPONSE OF MY COMMANDS SENT TO MY SNIPER'S INITIAL MOVEMENTS WERE NON RESPONSIVE AND IRRATIC SOME OF THE TIME, RESULTING IN GETTING ME KILLED, OR I WOULD HAVE TO REPEATLY ACTIVE THE COMMAND TO GET IT TO WORK, ALONG WITH HAVING THE TROUBLE OF THROWING GERNADES, IT CAN BE VERY FRUSTRATING! I INITIALLY USED THE WIRELESS REMOTE, THEN I TRIED MY CORD/WIRED REMOTE TO THE CONSOLE AND HAD BETTER RESPONSE RESULTS OVERALL. THE PS2 VERSION OF "SNIPER ELITE" HAD THIS SAME HAND CONTROLLER RESPONSE PROBLEM AND UNFORTUNATELY THIS IS PASSED ONTO "SNIPER ELITE V2". IT WOULD HAVE BEEN NICE TO HAVE A "MELEE" OPTION FOR THE CLOSE ENCOUNTERS I DID HAVE WITH THE ENEMY WHEN I LEAST EXPECTED IT, THUS THE FOUR STAR OVERALL RATING.
I REALLY ENJOYED "SNIPER ELITE V2" AND FOUND IT TO BE VERY CHALLENGING AND FUN!! IT'S SNIPER VS. SNIPERS IN THE WAR TORN AND BOMBED OUT CITY OF BERLIN (GERMANY) IN 1945. IT'S THE SNIPER CHARACTER I PORTRAY (FPS) VS. THE GERMAN "NAZI" ARMY DEFENDING THEIR LAST HOPES, AND THE AGGRESSIVE SOVIET "RED" ARMY SEIZING CONTROL OF THE CITY ITSELF AND THE GERMAN TECHNOLOGY ALONG WITH IT. PLUS A SECRET DEAL HAS BEEN AGREED UPON AND ARRANGED THAT I MUST DEFINETLY PUT AN END TOO BEFORE, WELL YOU GET THE IDEA......
IF YOU LIKE (FPS) "SNIPER / BLACK OPS" VIDEO GAMES AS I DO, THEN YOU'LL ENJOY THIS ONE, AND I RECCOMEND IT!!
LOCK N' LOAD YOUR WALTHER/GEWEHR43 SEMI AUTOMATIC SNIPER RIFLE, ADJUST YOUR ZF42 OPTIC SIGHT FOR WIND AND DISTANCE AND FIRE, BECAUSE THE CLEAN AND PERFECT SHOT YOU JUST MADE (IN THIS GAME) WILL CERTAINLY REWARD YOU WITH THE STUNNING GRAPHICS OF THE IMPACT IN/ON/OF YOUR TARGET!
top_screaming.title: I REALLY ENJOYED "SNIPER ELITE V2" !!

top_screaming.text:
I JUST RECEIVED THE COLLECTORS EDITION AND THE GAMEPLAY FIGHTING IS AMAZING. THE GRAPHICS, SOUND AND ATTENTION TO EACH UNIQUE CHARACTER'S POWER IS VERY WELL DONE. THE DRAG COMES WHEN YOU REALIZE THE STORY IS VERY WEAK AND THE AMOUNT OF TIME IN BETWEEN FIGHTS IS RIDICULOUS...THE LEAD UP INTRO INTO EACH FIGHT IS TOO LONG. i WISH YOU COULD SKIP THE DIALOG BETWEEN THE CHARACTER ONLY BECAUSE SINCE THE STORY IS SO WEAK YOU FIND YOURSELF NOT INTERESTED IN THE PLOT OF THE CUBES AND THE CONQUERING OF THE GALAXY. THE CHARACTERS REMAINED SO ONE DIMENSIONAL AS THEY DON'T GIVE EACH CHARACTER A PERSONALIZED PART IN THE OVERALL STORY ARC. YOU WILL GET BORED FAST BUT THE GRAPHICS AND FIGHTS ARE VERY COOL TO LOOK AT IF YOU ARE AN ANIME FAN.

THE ONLY DOWNSIDE IS THAT I PURCHASED THE COLLECTOR'S EDITION BECAUSE I WANTED THE AMAZING GAME BUT I ALSO DECIDED TO PAY PREMIUM PRICE FOR THE STATUE, ART BOARDS AND THE COLLECTOR'S GAME CASE. THE CHARACTER COSTUME DOWNLOADS WAS A NICE TOUCH BUT NOT A DEAL MAKER.

THE ART BOARDS ARE VERY NICE AND THE COLOR JUST POPS OFF OF THE ART BOARD. THE DRAWING IS ALSO WELL DONE, LUFFY LOOKS A LITTLE OFF WITH THE ANGLE OF THE NECK BUT OVERALL EACH ART BOARD IS VERY NICE.

THE COLLECTOR'S GAME CASE IS PRETTY COOL TOO, IT'S A NICE GRAPHIC AND SOMETHING ABOUT THE ALUMINUM AND THE HEFTY WEIGHT ALWAYS GIVE IT A GREAT FEEL. THE COSTUME DOWNLOADS WERE PRETTY COOL BUT NOTHING THAT WILL BLOW YOUR HAIR BACK.

I PAID THE PRICE FOR THE DIORAMA AS I AM A HUGE COLLECTOR AND I WAS VERY, VERY DISAPPOINTED! IT IS A HOLLOW BASE AND THE CHARACTERS ARE ALSO VERY LIGHTWEIGHT AND SEEM HOLLOWED OUT AS WELL. IT IS PRETTY MUCH LOW LEVEL PLASTIC FIGURES THAT ALMOST FEEL LIKE SQUEEZE TOYS. THE COLOR SCHEME IS ALSO BLAND, THE ONLY REDEEMING QUALITY IS THE DESIGN OF THE CHARACTER AS THEY LOOK LIKE THE CHARACTER THEY WERE MODELLED AFTER.

I WOULD NOT GIVE IT SUCH A LOW RATING IF I HAD PURCHASED THE GAME ALONE FOR REGULAR PRICE BUT I WAS WILLING TO PAY $259 FOR THE PRETTY MUCH THE DIORAMA AND THE ART BOARDS. IF THE GAME WAS $60 THEN I DEFINITELY DON'T SEE THE DIORAMA, STEEL CASE AND THE ART BOARDS TO BE WORTH AN ADDITIONAL $200. IT WAS A VERY DISAPPOINTING PURCHASE, PART OF THE JOY OF THE GAME WAS MARRED BY THE CHEAPNESS OF THE DIORAMA.

I DEFINITELY DO NOT RECOMMEND THE COLLECTORS EDITION!!
top_screaming.title: QUALITY IS NOT WORTH THE COLLECTORS EDITION!

top_exclaim.text:
OK. Everyone, or most, seem to think that there are several problems with Pikmin. Everyone thinks that the game's problems are:
-The thirty day time limit
-The lack or replayability
-Having only 3 types of pikmin
-Each day being only 15 minutes
-The Pikmin are hard to control
-The game is too short
Those are the main problems i have read over the reviews of this game. Even if you don't agree with all of those problems i'm sure there's atleast one on that list that you might kind of agree with. If so, let me explain. The thirty day time limit is a way to help you be organized and without it the game would be pointless. When i played the game for the first time i thought it could be frustrating at times but it made me think ahead and i could sometimes even feel my adrenaline pumping because i had less days than i had parts to get.
The lack of replayability does not even exist with this game. I don't understand what replayability is if this game does not have it. If you look up to the manufacturer's review or some reveiew up at the top it says one of the game's strengths is the nonlinear strategic gameplay. That means that you can play all the levels and the tasks required to beat the game in any way you want. For example on day 7 one person might be in the forest naval getting a part when someone else choses to stay in the last are a getting a different part. This is because of the nonlinear gameplay.you could get none or 5 parts in a day. The order of parts isn't even set. This means that there must be thousands of different ways to beat this game. Including how many different days you could beat it in. You could beat the game a second or third time to beat the game in less days. I will be honest that i played and have beaten this game 30 times probably by now.I did that and every time it was different.
People also say it has not enough kinds of pikmin. i think three is the perfect number. And notice the colors(all yellow, red, and blue which are the prime clors meaning you can make any color with those three colors). Each one has it's own ability too. If you still think there should be more just wait for pikmin 2 to come out. there's going to be skinny albinos and pudgey purples.
I also think that each day being 15 minutes was fine. If you think about what your going to do and concentrate you can get up to 4 or 5 parts a day. I have done it and i know it's possible because i have beaten the game in 9 days probably over a dozen times. If you just mess up you can always restart and it's not the end of the world. I also hear people saying that restarting the levels are a pain because they run out of time in each day, but if "each day is so short" like they also say then what's the big deal?!
Another thing that people say is that it is hard to control. i must agree that there are a lot of controls, yet it didn't take me long at all to get all of the controls down. I think the more ontrols the better because each control is helpful when attacking enemies. And the constant need of adjusting the camera just means you always have a good angle.
i do agree that the game could be longer but it doesn't really bother me that much since i think the replay value is so high. THIS GAME ROCKS!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
top_exclaim.title: Why doesn't Everyone love this game!!!!!??????
